inputs: 
    die: the die object
    fuses: list, uses pysv search to return a list of matching fields
    exclude: list, of keywords to exclude from the search, like fivr, virtual
    include: list, of keywords to include from the search, like cbopair
    parent_delim: lambda function used to determine the name of each row, usually a parent or substring of the fields being searched
    baseForSearch: base path for the pysv search

functions.name: harass_rc_cfcmem_dts
functions.doc:
Randomly inject temperatures into RC CFCMEM DTS sensors over a period of time.

Based on EMTTM sighting 22022311653 analysis which identified RC CFCMEM sensor behavior.
Uses DFX override method: sensor_instances_X.dfx_enabled and dfx_temperature

Args:
    runtime_seconds: Total time to run harassment loop (default: 60 seconds)injection_delay-
    temp_min: Minimum temperature in Celsius (default: 20)
    temp_max: Maximum temperature in Celsius (default: 100)
    imh_num_list: List of IMH instances to target (default: [0] or all IMHs)
    socket: Socket number (default: 0)
    injection_delay: Delay between injections in seconds (default: 0.0 for max speed)
                    Set to 0 for no delay, > 0 to slow down injection rate

RC CFCMEM sensor instances from thermal_topology.hpp:
    - UCIE sensors: 32,33,34, 37,38,39, 41,42,43, 44,45,46,47,48,49 (UCIE_B/A/C/IMH_UCIE0/1)
    - DDR sensors: 62,63,64 (UCIE_D)
    - CGU: 65

functions.name: calc_eff_tjmax
functions.doc:
//Offset calc
eff_tj_max_c1e_offset       = c1e_disabled                      ? fuse.TJ_MAX_C1E_DISABLED_OFFSET           : 0
eff_tj_max_msr_offset       = MSR.IA32_TEMPERATURE_TARGET[TJ_MAX_TCC_OFFSET]
eff_tj_max_overall_offset   = eff_tj_max_c1e_offset + eff_tj_max_msr_offset

//eff TjMax calc
eff_tj_max_sst              = CR.SST_PP_CONTROL[FEATURE_STATE][0]  ? SST_BF_CONFIG_[sst_pp_level]_T_THROTTLE   : CR.SST_PP_[sst_pp_level]_T_THROTTLE
eff_tj_max                  = eff_tj_max_sst - eff_tj_max_overall_offset

functions.name: set_thrt_levels

Description: Sets the thermal throttle levels for memory channels and subchannels.
            This function updates the thermal throttle levels for memory channels and subchannels based on the provided threshold values.
            If no specific memory channel or subchannel is provided, it updates all available channels and subchannels.
Parameters:
    mc (RegisterComponent | None): The memory channel object. If None, all memory channels will be updated.
    dimm (RegisterComponent | None): The dimm object. If None, all dimms will be updated.
    value (int | None): The combined threshold value. If None, individual threshold values (low, mid, high, crit) will be used.
    low (int): The temperature for the low threshold. Default is 0xff.
    mid (int): The temperature for the mid threshold. Default is 0xff.
    high (int): The temperature for the high threshold. Default is 0x4c.
    crit (int): The temperature for the critical threshold. Default is 0x3.
    log (tool.EasyLogger): Logger object for logging information. Default is _log.

    pop_mc (RegisterComponent | None): Memory controller register component object. Required parameter.
    log (tool.EasyLogger): Logger instance for logging messages. Default is the module logger.

Returns:
    int: The register data value if successful, -1 if pop_mc is None or on error.

module_doc:
PMX script for SST-BF harassment and validation for GNR.


Functions:
    No functions
Classes:
    sstBFPmx
Main in this file

classes.name: sstBFPmx
classes.doc:
Class for testing SST-BF on PMX.

Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx.content.sst_pp_pmx.sstPmxBase.

Note that pmx_lib2.ProcessHanlder uses optparse (depreciated in python) rather than argparse.
Example usage if running from this module rather than runPmx:
    - opt_parser = dynamicSS.get_options()
    - (options, leftover_args) = opt_parser.parse_args(args)
    - main_test = sstPPPmx(options)
    - main_test.testLoop()

Args:
    options = (option obj) Option object from instantiated parser object from sstPmxBase.get_options method

Methods:
    [see diamondrapids.pm.pmx.content.sst_pp_pmx.sstPmxBase for non-overridden methods]
    - _log_file_dates   :(overrides parent) Log the last edited dates for all needed files
    - loopSetup         :(overrides parent) Set up for the BF test
    - mainTest          :(overrides parent) The main test to do on each iteration
    - loopCleanup       :(overrides parent) Clean up for the BF test
    - _check_bf_fuses   :Check to see if the BF fuses are meeting the fuse rules
    - _verify_change    :Check to see if the BF enablement was honored on the component
    - _verify_reject    :Check to see if the BF enablement was rejected and an error on the TPMI register was present
    - _verify_info      :Check to see if BF functioned correctly

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc: Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.content.sst_pp_pmx.sstPmxBase.

methods.name: loopSetup
methods.doc:
Sets up tests before doing main test loop.

Overrides method diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.loopSetup.
One of the first methods called in sstBFPmx.testLoop method (inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.testLoop).

Args:
    No arguments

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: mainTest
methods.doc:
Main test that will be looped.

Overrides method diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.mainTest.
Will be continued to be looped in sstBFPmx.testLoop method (inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.testLoop) until 
end of test duration or a failure. If harassing, will randomly enable/disable every iteration provided that BF is
available on the current PP level. If validating, will verify that all BF functionality is correct. If negative test case,
will try to enable BF, which should be rejected and give an error on TPMI.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: loopCleanup
methods.doc:
Cleans up test loop by returning SST to initial state.

Overrides method diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.loopCleanup.
One of the methods called in sstBFPmx.testLoop method (inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.testLoop) when either
all loops of the main test are complete, or if exiting from print_and_exit method. Restores BF to initial state stored in
loopSetup method.

Args:

classes.name: sstPmxBase
classes.doc:
Base class for PMX scripts for SST.

Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.ProcessHandler.

Note that pmx_lib2.ProcessHanlder uses optparse (depreciated in python) rather than argparse.
Example usage if running from this module rather than runPmx:
    - opt_parser = dynamicSS.get_options()
    - (options, leftover_args) = opt_parser.parse_args(args)
    - main_test = sstPmxBase(opt_dict)
    - main_test.testLoop()

Args:
    - options       = (option obj) Option object from instantiated parser object from sstPmxBase.get_options method
    - sstfeature    = (str) The SST feature this class is being used for, one of [pp, cp, bf, tf]

Methods:
    [see diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.ProcessHandler for non-overridden methods]
    - get_options           :(static, overrides parent) Adds options for SST tests. See parent for base PMX options.
    - _logmessage           :Send a message to the relevant log
    - _log_file_dates       :Log all the file versions for debug
    - _iterate_over_comp    :Use a function over all components
    - _iterate_over_socket  :Use a function over all sockets
    - _get_core_map         :Update the core map method and log the core mapping
    - _intepret_state       :(static) Interpret an int as a state string
    - _get_initial          :Get the initial state of the component and save in attributes
    - get_initial           :Get the initial state of all sockets
    - _get_current          :Get the current state of the component and save in attributes
    - get_current           :Get the current state of all sockets
    - _update_cap           :Get the SST capability of the component and save in attributes
    - _do_test_for_feature  :Check if the component has the capability to do the test
    - _log_test_error       :Log the error list of a test
    - _verify_tpmi          :Check if the TPMI registers are populated correctly on the component
    - _sample_core_freq     :Sample the core frequencies and update the rolling average
    - exit_sst_test         :Exit the SST test within the PMX framework

methods.name: get_options
methods.doc:
Creates options parser for sstPmxBase() instantiation.

Static Method.
See diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.ProcessHandler.get_options() for all default options. Overrides get_options method in 
parent class and adds the additional options: --stubmode, --verify, --threshold, --harass, --omitpcudata.

Args:
    parser = (python built-in OptionParser, optional) Some other parser you wish to inherit from. Default=None.

Returns:
    (Obj) A get_options() parser object.

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc:
Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx_lib2.ProcessHandler.

Unique attributes:
    - _sstfeature           = (str) Which feature this class is used for
    - already_looped_once   = (bool) If looping once, the variable to see if we have already looped this test run.
    - iter_count            = (int) How many iterations have occurred
    - change_count          = (int) Current iteration of changing state, does not increment every loop
    - error_count           = (int) Running total of verification failures
    - states                = (dict) All subcomponents of each socket. Ex. socket1.cbb0.
        - Key           | Value
        - {socket_name} | (dict) Index and dies
            - Key           | Value
            - {die_name}    | (dict) Dictionary for this die
                - Key   | Value
                - ini   | (dict) The initial state of the dies that is restored after the end of the test
                    - Key   | Value
                    - feat  | (int) [b6:4 PP_level][b3 CP_en][b2 1 if ord, 0 if prop][b1 BF_en][b0 TF_en]
                    - clos  | (dict) CLOS IDs of the cores on cbb or (None) on imh
                - curr  | (dict) Current state of the dies, same as above
            - cap           | (dict) Capability dict of the socket
            - do_tests      | (bool) If tests should be performed on the socket
            - available     | (list of int) PP levels available for the test
            - targ          | (int) Target value for tests
            - boot_level    | (tuple) (int) The boot PP level, (bool) If override tried to set a level not available
    - coretype              = (str) Core type from registers.py method core_type
    - core_map_obj          = (coreMap object) Core mapping object from sst_utils (coreMap class)
    - core_history          = (coreHistory object) Frequency history of all the cores from sst_validate (coreHistory class)
    - failing_comp          = (dict) All the components that failed their test
    - negative_test         = (bool) If the negative test should be done instead

methods.name: _logmessage
methods.doc:
Write a log message to logger in pmx_lib2.ProcessHandler. Since the log is hard-fixed at level info, the lower levels
have to be adapted to the info level.

Args:
    - log_level = (str) Level to log the message at. One of the default levels [debug, info, warning, error, critical] or new levels added
                    in svtools/logging/toolbox.py [verbose, result, success, debugall, access]
    - message   = (str) Message to add to the log

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: _log_file_dates
methods.doc:
Will put the last edited date of all necessary files. Will allow quicker debug of files not being the the correct version.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: _iterate_over_comp
methods.doc:
Will apply the specified function/method across all components on all sockets.

The function must have the socket and die names as args and return an int. Will skip applying the
function over dies that are not in the filter_list. If you are testing functionality in stub mode,
set run_only_once to True.

Args:
    - rep_function  = (function or method) Function/method that will be executed repeatedly on each component. The function/method must have
                        the format func(socket_name, die_name) or func(socket_name, die_name, rep_arg). The function/method should return an
                        int which is the count of errors that occured on execution.
    - filter_list   = (list of str, optional) Will only apply the function if the die name is in this list. Default=['imh', 'cbb'].
    - run_only_once = (bool, optional) If instead of iterating over all components, apply the specified function only on the very first
                        component. Default=False.
    - rep_args      = (any, optional) Any additional arg for rep_function. Default=None.

Returns:
    (int) The cumulative sum of all int returns from each iteration of rep_function

methods.name: _iterate_over_socket
methods.doc:
Will apply the specified function/method across all sockets.

The function must have the socket name as args and return an int. If you are testing functionality in stub mode,
set run_only_once to True.

Args:
    - rep_function  = (function or method) Function/method that will be executed repeatedly on each socket. The function/method must have
                        the format func(socket_name) or func(socket_name, rep_arg). The function/method should return an int which is the
                        count of errors that occured on execution.
    - run_only_once = (bool, optional) If instead of iterating over all components, apply the specified function only on the very first
                        component. Default=False.
    - rep_args      = (any, optional) Any additional arg for rep_function. Default=None.

Returns:
    (int) The cumulative sum of all int returns from each iteration of rep_function

methods.name: _get_core_map
methods.doc:
Will determine the best core mapping on the specified cbb die and print that information to the log. Used to update
attribute core_map_obj.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) The socket to get core mapping
    - die_name      = (str) The cbb die of the specified socket

Returns:
    (int) 1 if not a cbb die, otherwise 0

methods.name: _intepret_state
methods.doc:
Creates a string which is the intpretation of attribute states[socket][die][ini/curr][feat].

Args:
    state_val = (int) The value that is to be interpreted

Returns:
    (str) The intepretation of the state var

methods.name: _get_initial
methods.doc:
Will get the current state of all SST features and save this in attribute states. Will store both on initial key and
current key.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) The socket to get the initial state of SST
    - die_name      = (str) The die of the specified socket

Returns:
    (int) 1 if there isn any error, otherwise 0

methods.name: get_initial
methods.doc:
Will get the current state of all SST features and save this in attribute states. Will store both on ini key and
curr key.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int) The number of errors on iterating over all dies

methods.name: _get_current
methods.doc:
Will get the current state of all SST features and save this in attribute states.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) The socket to get the current state of SST
    - die_name      = (str) The die of the specified socket
    - print_only    = (str, optional) Have the log messages only print for certain dies. One of the following:
        - if "all": states of all dies will be logged. Default.
        - if "cbb": state of all cbbs will be logged
        - if "imh": state of all ios will be logged

Returns:
    (int) 1 if there isn any error, otherwise 0

methods.name: get_current
methods.doc:
Will get the current state of all SST features and save this in attribute states. Will store only on curr key.

Args:
    - socket_name = (str, optional) The socket to get the current state of SST. If None, will iterate over all sockets.
    - die_name    = (str, optional) The die of the specified socket. If None, will iterate over all dies.
    - print_only = (str, optional) Have the log messages only print for certain dies. One of the following:
        - if "all": states of all dies will be logged. Default.
        - if "cbb": state of all cbbs will be logged
        - if "imh": state of all ios will be logged.

Returns:
    (int) The number of errors on iterating over all dies

methods.name: _update_cap
methods.doc:
Will get SST capability of the specified socket use that information to update attribute self.states

Args:
    - socket_name = (str) Name of the socket to get capability

Returns:
    (int) 1 if there is any errors and thus self.states is not updated, otherwise 0

methods.name: _do_test_for_feature
methods.doc:
Will see if specified socket is capable for the specified feature so that tests on that feature can be executed.

Args:
    - socket_name = (str) Name of the socket to check capability

Returns:
    (int) 1 if the socket is to be tested, otherwise 0

methods.name: _log_test_error
methods.doc:
Will log the error list on the specified socket+die. Will not log if error_list is empty.

Args:
    - cname         = (str) Name of the component that the error list came from
    - error_list    = (list of str) The errors on specified component

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: _verify_tpmi
methods.doc:
Will check if the TPMI registers on all dies of the specified socket are populated with the right value.

Args:
    - socket_name = (str) Name of the socket to check TPMI registers
    - die_name    = (str) Name of the die to check TPMI registers
Returns:
    (int) The number of mismatched fields on the socket

methods.name: _sample_core_freq
methods.doc:
Samples all core frequencies and returns the average frequencies.

Args:
    No args

Returns: (dict) The average frequencies
    - Key                   | Value
    - {str of socket name}  | (dict) All cbbs on this socket
        - Key                   | Value
        - {str of cbb name} | (dict) All cores on this cbb
            - Key                   | Value
            - {str of core name}    | (int) The average frequency of this core

methods.name: exit_sst_test
methods.doc:
After putting necessary information in the logs, will use print_and_exit method to end the test.

Args:
    - failure_type = (str) What failure occured. One of [setup, tpmi, bootmask, change, function, fuse]

Returns:
    No return value

classes.name: validatePP
classes.doc:
Checks necessary values/fuses to validate that SST-PP is working properly.

Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.sst.sstUtils.validateSS

Args:
    - option_dict   = (dict) Option dict from instantiated parser object from validateSS.get_options method.
    - with_handler  = (bool, optional) If the logger should automatically come with a file/log handler. If False, the user is
                        expected to add their own file handler. Default=True.

Methods: [see diamondrapids.pm.sst.sstUtils.validateSS for non-overridden methods]
    - test_bios_honored         :check if BIOS settings were honored
    - test_pp_fuses_logical     :check if fuses are logical
    - test_single_tf_per_pp     :check if there is only one TF configuration per PP level
    - test_tpmi_regs            :check if the values in the TPMI registers are correct
    - test_boot_core_mask       :check if the boot core mask was honored
    - _goto_pp_level            :change the PP level on a socket and update attributes
    - test_tpmi_control         :check if TPMI controls are honored
    - test_pp_functionality     :check if all expected acitons of PP are functioning correctly
    - test_hgs_update           :check if HGS is updating on PP level change
    - test_non_boot_core_mask   :check if core mask is honored on PP level change

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc:
Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.sst.sstUtils.validateSS

Args:
    - option_dict   = (dict) Option dict from instantiated parser object from validateSS.get_options method.
    - with_handler  = (bool, optional) If the logger should automatically come with a file/log handler. If False, the user is
                        expected to add their own file handler. Default=True.

Unique attributes:
    - component_dict    = (dict, overrides parent) Info of each socket+die
        - Key           | Value
        - {socket_name} | (dict) Dict of all subcomponents of each socket
            - Key           | Value
            - {die_name}    | (dict) Info of this die
                - Key           | Value
                - do_tests      | (bool) If certain tests should be done on this component
                - capability    | (dict) Capability of the component
                - There will be other values in this dict used specifically for certain methods
    - bootlevel         = (int) The boot PP level of the device

methods.name: test_bios_honored
methods.doc:
Will check if BIOS settings assigned during boot are honored.

Args:
    No args

Returns: (tuple)
    - (int) Number of components that had flex ratios incorrect,
    - (int) If static boot setting, number of components that had DESIRED_CORE_MASK incorrect)

methods.name: test_pp_fuses_logical
methods.doc:
A logical check to see if fuse values for SST-PP are logically correct.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int) Number of checks that did not succeed, otherwise 0.

methods.name: test_single_tf_per_pp
methods.doc:
Not every PP level will have a matching TF configuration. If a level does have a TF config, it will have at most one. 
This test will ensure there is at most one TF config for each PP level.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int) Number of checks that failed, otherwise 0

methods.name: test_tpmi_regs
methods.doc:
Will check if TPMI registers are operating as intended. The value in these registers must match those of fuses and BIOS.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int) The number of errors while checking all TPMI regs, otherwise 0.

methods.name: test_boot_core_mask
methods.doc:
Test to check that all dies have the core mask of the boot PP level is being honored. Make sure that option
--biospp is correct (option sets attribute bootlevel).

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int) The number of components that did not honor the mask

methods.name: _goto_pp_level
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to change to specified PP level on specified socket. Will keep levels unchanged if the socket is incapable
of going to that level. Will also update component_dict[socket][die][do_tests]. Note that this will also disable BF and TF.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) Name of the socket to change PP levels. Default=socket0
    - pp_level      = (int) The level to change to. Default=imh0

Returns:
    (int) -1 if any components failed to go to the PP level, otherwise 0

methods.name: test_tpmi_control
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to change PP levels of the device. If the die is unable to go to the intended PP level,
the test will check if the level is disabled or if dynamic switching is not allowed. If the level change
does not occur because of these two, then the die failed the test.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int) The number of dies that failed to honor TPMI control

methods.name: test_pp_functionality
methods.doc:
Test to see if SST-PP functions as intended. If static, will only check functionality on boot level. If dynamic, 
will iterate over all PP levels.

Args:
    - force_turbo_enabled   = (bool, optional) If writes to CRs should occur to force turbo enabled. Target values do
                                depend on if turbo is enabled or not. Default=False.
    - turbo_disable_instead = (bool, optional) If turbo should be forced disabled instead. Will do nothing if
                                force_turbo_enabled == False. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) The number of parameters that were incorrect

methods.name: test_hgs_update
methods.doc:
Harrdware-Guided Schedluing HGS builds the Hardware Feedback Info HFI table in memory which reports the performance
capability of all cores. HGS also offlines cores by writing 0 to the perf of that core. On a PP-level change, HGS should:
- Update performance to match HWP_CAPABILITY.HIGHEST_PERFORMANCE (which is automatically changed on PP-level change)
- Offline/reactivate cores to match the core count/mask of the PP-level

Test will iterate through all PP levels, then check that the HFI table is updating as intended. Dynamic-PP needs to be
enabled in BIOS for this test to work.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int) The number of sockets that failed the check over all PP-levels

methods.name: test_non_boot_core_mask
methods.doc:
The boot PP level determines which cores are offlined according to the core mask. When changing to a PP level that
is not the boot level, the core mask is honored in a different way. Cores that are online are treated normally, but the
cores that are offlined are done so not by disabling the core but by setting its max freq/best performance to the min 
freq pm. Will iterate over all cores to see if the mask is honored:
- Online cores should have ia32_hwp.maximum_performance == highest TRL of the PP level
- Offline cores should have ia32_hwp.maximum_performance == min freq pm for the cores

Will perform this check over all sockets+cbb dies

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int) The number of cores that did not honor the mask

functions.name: check_flex_ratio
functions.doc:
BIOS must ensure that flex ratio = min(BIOS option, SST-PP P1). Will check that the flex ratio is correct on each
socket+die per PP level.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, otherwise the failed checks on all PP levels of the specified device

functions.name: check_module_mask
functions.doc:
Check if BIOS set resolved core mask correctly to the boot PP level.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, 1 if the mask was not set correctly, otherwise 0

functions.name: check_pp_fuses_logical
functions.doc:
Will check if the PP fuses are logically correct. If incorrect, there may be errors in functionality. Will check the
following four items on the specified socket+die:
- If core enable masks are subsets of the PP level with more cores (e.g. PP1 cores are a subset of PP0 cores)
- If the P1 ratios of each instruction is decreasing
- If the turbo ratio numcore for each bucket is increasing
- If the turbo ratio limits for each bucket is decreasing
- If the turbo ratio limits for each cdyn/license is decreasing

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this fuse check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - report_mismatch   = (bool, optional) If the list of errors should also be returned. Default=False.
    - log               = (Logger, optional) Logger object to use. Default=_log.

Returns:
    - (int) the number of fuses that were incorrect, otherwise 0

functions.name: check_tpmi_matches_fuses
functions.doc:
Will see if the SST-PP TPMI registers that simply report fused values match their intended values on the specified 
socket+die. Note that imh dies have sst_pp_info_4 to _10 populated with zeros.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - report_mismatch   = (bool, optional) If the error list should also be returned. Default=False.
    - map_obj           = (coreMap object, optional) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, otherwise the number of TPMI fields that were incorrectly set

functions.name: check_pp_functionality
functions.doc:
Will see if the SST-PP functionality is working as intended. Checks various parameters that PP influences (e.g. thermals,
uncore frequency, turbo ratios) matches the information reported in the TPMI registers sst_pp_info_#.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level          = (int, optional) If Default=-1, will automatically get PP level. Otherwise the level to get TPMI info
    - return_messages   = (str, optional) What messages to return. One of the following. Default=none.
        - none  = have no messages returned
        - error = have only errors returned
        - all   = have both successes and errors returned
    - coretype          = (str, optional) The type of core of the system. One of [bigcore, atom]. Default=bigcore.
    - map_obj           = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.
    - hist_obj          = (coreHistory object) The history of all core frequencies if you want to check by averages. If Default=None, will do
                            normal one time sample. Otherwise will get frequency from this object.

Returns: (tuple)
    - (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, otherwise the number of PP functionality errors
    - (list of str) The return messages, depending on arg return_messages
        - none  = no messages, will be an empty list []
        - error = only error messages, will be an empty list if there are no errors
        - all   = all messages, both passes and failures

functions.name: check_single_tf
functions.doc:
Will check the specified socket+die that each PP level will have at most one TF config.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to check TF configs. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (int) How many PP levels had greater than 1 TF config, otherwise 0

functions.name: check_hfi
functions.doc:
SST-PP works with and without Hardware Guided Scheduling HGS. HGS uses a table in DRAM called the Hardware Feedback Info 
HFI table that pcode is responsible for populating. SST-PP only updates the performance column of the HFI table.
- For active cores, the table value should be == hwp_capabilities.highest_performance
- For idled cores, the table value should be 0

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to check HFI table. Default=socket0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) PP level of dies on the socket. If Default=-1, will automatically get PP level.

Return: (dict) The result of the HGS check
    - Key           | Value
    - count         | Result of the core count check
    - err           | Number of values in the table incorrect
    - target        | Target value for the active cores
    - {int of core} | Performance value in the HFI table for this core

functions.name: check_boot_core_mask
functions.doc:
The boot PP level determines which cores are offlined. This check will use fuse sst_pp_{boot level}_module_disable_mask to
determine which cores should be disabled. Will then iterate over all cores to see if the mask is honored (cores that should
be online/offline are online/offline).

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to perform this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - map_obj       = (coreMap object, optional) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any access errors, otherwise the number of cores that did not honor the mask

functions.name: check_non_boot_core_mask
functions.doc:
The boot PP level determines which cores are offlined according to the core mask. When changing to a PP level that is
not the boot level, the core mask is honored in a different way. Cores that are online are treated normally, but the cores
that are offlined are done so not by disabling the core but by setting its max freq/best performance to the min freq pm.
Will iterate over all cores to see if the mask is honored:
- Online cores should have ia32_hwp.maximum_performance == highest TRL of the PP level
- Offline cores should have ia32_hwp.maximum_performance == min freq pm for the cores

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to perform this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - report_error  = (bool, optional) If the error list should also be returned. Default=False.

Returns:
    - If report_error: (tuple)
        - (int) -1 if there are any access errors, otherwise the number of cores that did not honor the mask,
        - (list of str) error list
    - Else:
        - (int) Same int as above

functions.name: _debug_core_mask
functions.doc:
Visual prints to help with the debug of core masks.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get information from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - map_obj       = (coreMap object, optional) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns:
    No return value

functions.name: pp_debug
functions.doc:
Debug script to run when triaging some bug with SST-PP.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get relevant information from. If Default='', will iterate over
                        all sockets.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die on the specified socket. If Default='', will iterate over all dies on
                        the specified socket.

Returns:
    (list of str) All debug info

functions.name: get_pp_lvls_info
functions.doc:
Get SST-PP information for the specified socket and die(s).

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get relevant information from. If Default='', will iterate over
                        all sockets.
    - die_name      = (list | str, optional) Name of the die on the specified socket. If Default='', will iterate over all dies on
                        the specified socket.
    - log           = (EasyLogger, optional) Logger object. Default=_log.

Returns:
    (lis of PrettyTable) PrettyTable object with all the SST-PP information for each die on the specified socket.

functions.name: main
functions.doc:
Main function that builds parser and runs test on validatePP class.

Example if running in python (NOT command line):
    import diamondrapids.pm.Active_PM.SST.sst_pp.sst_pp_validate as sstpp
    sstpp.main('-l warning --dynamicpp'.split())

Args:

                        argument will override command line arguments. See diamondrapids.pm.sst.lib.sst_utils.validateSS.get_options() for
                        possible options.
    - log_path      = (str, optional) The path you want the logs to go to if you do not want to use the default log

Returns:

classes.name: sstTFPmx
classes.doc:
Class for testing SST-TF on PMX.

Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx.content.sst_pp_pmx.sstPmxBase.

Note that pmx_lib2.ProcessHanlder uses optparse (depreciated in python) rather than argparse.
Example usage if running from this module rather than runPmx:
    - opt_parser = dynamicSS.get_options()
    - (options, leftover_args) = opt_parser.parse_args(args)
    - main_test = sstTFPmx(options)
    - main_test.testLoop()

Args:
    options = (option obj) Option object from instantiated parser object from sstPmxBase.get_options method

Methods:
    [see diamondrapids.pm.pmx.content.sst_pp_pmx.sstPmxBase for non-overridden methods]
    - _log_file_dates   :(overrides parent) Log the last edited dates for all needed files
    - loopSetup         :(overrides parent) Set up for the TF test
    - mainTest          :(overrides parent) The main test to do on each iteration
    - loopCleanup       :(overrides parent) Clean up for the TF test
    - _check_tf_fuses   :Check if the TF fuses follow the fuse rules
    - _verify_change    :Check to see if the TF enablement was honored on the component
    - _verify_reject    :Check to see if the TF enablement was rejected and an error on the TPMI register was present
    - _verify_info      :Check to see if TF functioned correctly

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc: Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.content.sst_pp_pmx.sstPmxBase.

methods.name: loopSetup
methods.doc:
Sets up tests before doing main test loop.

Overrides method diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.loopSetup.
One of the first methods called in sstTFPmx.testLoop method (inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.testLoop).

Args:
    No arguments

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: mainTest
methods.doc:
Main test that will be looped.

Overrides method diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.mainTest.
Will be continued to be looped in sstTFpmx.testLoop method (inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.testLoop) until 
end of test duration or a failure. If harassing, will randomly enable/disable every iteration provided that TF is
available on the current PP level. If validating, will verify that all TF functionality is correct. If negative test case,
will try to enable TF, which should be rejected an give an error on TPMI.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: loopCleanup
methods.doc:
Cleans up test loop by returning SST to initial state.

Overrides method diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.loopCleanup.
One of the methods called in sstTFPmx.testLoop method (inherited from diamondrapids.pm.pmx.pmx_lib2.testLoop) when either
all loops of the main test are complete, or if exiting from print_and_exit method. Restores TF to initial state stored in
loopSetup method.

Args:
    No arguments

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: _check_tf_fuses

Will check if the TF fuses are logically correct.

Returns:

Will read the reg SST_PP_STATUS of the specified die to see if TF honored enable/disable.

Args:

    - die_name      = (str) Name of the die of the socket

Returns:

methods.doc:
Will read the reg SST_PP_STATUS of the specified die to see if the TF enablement was rejected because TF is fuse disabled.
Should only be used for negative test.

Args:

Returns:

methods.doc:
Will do various checks to verify that SST-TF is operating properly on the specified socket+die.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) Name of the socket to verify info on
    - die_name      = (str) Name of the die of the specified socket

Returns:
    (int) The number of errors on the die

classes.name: validateTF
classes.doc:
Checks necessary values/fuses to validate that SST-TF is working properly.

Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.sst.sstUtils.validateSS

Args:
    :option_dict = (dict) Option dict from instantiated parser object from validateSS.get_options method.
    :with_handler = (bool, optional) If the logger should automatically come with a file/log handler. If False, the user
        is expected to add their own file handler. Default=True.

Methods:
    [see diamondrapids.pm.sst.sstUtils.validateSS for non-overridden methods]
    - get_options               :(static) Parses the options from command line. See parent.
    - test_tf_fuses_logical     :Test to see if the TF fuses meet the fuse rules
    - test_tpmi_regs            :Test to see if the TF TPMI register are populated correctly
    - _goto_sst_state           :Go to a specific PP level and TF enablement on a component
    - test_tpmi_control         :Test to see if TF enablement by TPMI registers are honored
    - _check_tf_on_comp         :See if TF is functioning correctly
    - test_tf_functionality     :Test to see if TF functions correctly during different CLOS ID assignments to cores
    - _spoof_numcore            :If in stubmode, populate numcore registers so testing can still be done
    - test_sweep_all_buckets    :Test to see if TF functions correctly across all TF bins
    - test_cross_turbo_disabled :Test to see if TF functions correctly even when turbo is disabled
    - test_cross_clos_ordered   :Test to see if TF functions when a certain CLOS group is unused

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc:
Inherited from diamondrapids.pm.sst.sstUtils.validateSS

Unique attributes:
    - component_dict    = (dict, overrides parent) Info of each socket+die
        - Key           | Value
        - {socket_name} | (dict) Dict of all subcomponents of each socket
            - Key           | Value
            - {die_name}    | (dict) Info of this die
                - Key           | Value
                - do_tests      | (bool) If certain tests should be done on this component
                - capability    | (dict) Capability of the component
                - There will be other values in this dict used specifically for certain methods
    - bootlevel         = (int) The boot PP level of the device

A logical check to see if fuse values for SST-TF are logically correct.

Will check if TPMI registers are operating as intended.

The value in these registers must match those of fuses and BIOS.

methods.name: _goto_sst_state

Uses TPMI to change the SST state on specified socket. Will go to a specified PP level and have TF enabled/disabled.
Note this will disable SST-BF.

Args:

    - target_state  = (tuple) (int) PP level to change to, (bool) If TF should be enabled

Returns:

Will use TPMI to enable/disable SST-TF and see if the controls are honored.

TF should still be enabled if CP is not available on the component. Capability check is done through fuse
sst_cp_enable.

Test to see if SST-TF functions as intended. Depending on the test type, will assign certain cores to certain CLOS IDs.
If static PP, will only check functionality on boot level. If dynamic PP, will iterate over all PP levels.

Args:
    test_type = (str) What CLOS IDs are available for assignment. One of [all_zero, all_one, all_two, all_three, random_high,
                random_low, random].

Returns:
    (int) The number of errors for TF functionality

methods.name: _spoof_numcore

For stubmode, populate sst_tf_info_1 for tests. sst_tf_info_1 would be all zeros which would ruin certain tests.

    - die_name      = (str) Name of the die on the specified socket

Returns:

methods.name: test_sweep_all_buckets
methods.doc:
Test to see if SST-TF functions as intended. Depending on the test type, will assign certain cores to certain CLOS IDs.
If static PP, will only check functionality on boot level. If dynamic PP, will iterate over all PP levels.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int)

methods.name: test_cross_turbo_disabled
methods.doc:
Test to see if SST-TF functions as intended when turbo is disabled. Note that pcode will honor a TF enable although
turbo is disabled at runtime. See sst_tf_cross.py (test_cross_turbo_disabled method) for when turbo is disabled from boot.

If static PP, will only check functionality on boot level. If dynamic PP, will iterate over all PP levels.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (int) The number of errors for TF functionality

methods.name: test_cross_clos_ordered
methods.doc:
Cross product test of TF with CP ordered priority. Will enable TF and CP, and randomly assign CLOS IDs to all cores.
Specifically, this test will have one group of IDs will not be used for corner case checking. This is different than
test_tf_functionality method because this test enables CP while the aforementioned doesn't.

Args:
    (int) Which CLOS group to exclude.

Returns:
    (int) The number of cores that had the wrong frequency.

functions.name: check_tf_fuses_logical
functions.doc:
Will check if the TF fuses are logically correct. If incorrect, there may be errors in functionality. Will check the 
following five items on the specified socket+die:
- If the low priority LP ratio clips for increasing cdyn/license is non-increasing
- If the turbo ratio numcore for increasing bucket is non-decreasing
- If the turbo ratio limits for increasing bucket is non-increasing
- If the turbo ratio limits for increasing cdyn/license is non-increasing
- If SST-BF is enabled, TF LP ratio clip SSE >= BF p1 ratio SSE

DMR does not have cdyn 5 (the last index). All fuses for that index should be 0.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this fuse check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - report_mismatch   = (bool, optional) If the list of errors should also be returned. Default=False.
    - log               = (tool.EasyLogger, optional) Logger instance to use. Default=_log.

Returns:
    - If report_mismatch==False: (int) the number of fuses that were incorrect, otherwise 0
    - Else: (tuple) (int) same as above, (list of str) the list of all fuses that were incorrect

functions.name: check_tpmi_matches_fuses
functions.doc:
Will see if the SST-TF TPMI registers that simply report fused values match their intended values on the specified 
socket+die.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - report_mismatch   = (bool, optional) If the error list should also be returned. Default=False.
    - log               = (tool.EasyLogger, optional) Logger instance to use. Default=_log.

Returns:
    - If report_mismatch == False:
        (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, otherwise the number of TPMI fields that were incorrectly set
    - If report_mismatch == True: (tuple)
        - (int) Above,
        - (list of str) The list of all mismatched fields

functions.name: check_tf_functionality
functions.doc:
Will see if the SST-TF functionality is working as intended. Checks maximum frequencies are honored properly with
the information reported in the TPMI registers sst_tf_info_#.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level          = (int, optional) If Default=-1, will automatically get PP level. Otherwise the level to get TPMI info
    - return_messages   = (str, optional) What messages to return. One of the following. Default=none.
        - none  = have no messages returned
        - error = have only errors returned
        - all   = have both successes and errors returned
    - map_obj           = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns: (tuple)
    - (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, otherwise the number of TF functionality errors
    - (list of str) The return messages, depending on arg return_messages
        - none  = no messages, will be an empty list []
        - error = only error messages, will be an empty list if there are no errors
        - all   = all messages, both passes and failures

functions.name: get_offsets
functions.doc:
Will get the table offsets for each core on the compute die. The algorithm is:
    (Compute_Die_Instance << 6) + Module_Logical_ID + 2

Args:
    socket_num (int): The socket number
    cbb_num (int): The CBB number
Returns: (dict) The offsets per core
    - Key                   | Value
    - {logical ID of core}  | (int) Offset of that core in the table

functions.name: check_interruption
functions.doc:
Check if the HGS interruption is enabled

Args:
    socket_num (int): The socket number
Returns: (bool) True if the interruption is enabled

functions.name: enable_hgs
functions.doc:
Enables HGS

1. If HGS is supported (CPUID.6.EAX[19]), then the size of the Hardware Feedback Information sections (HFI) region is reported in CPUID6.EDX[11:8] (Represented as (number of 4K pages needed - 1))
2. SW (ex: OS Driver) will allocate the correct region size in memory and provide the Physical Address in IA32_HW_FEEDBACK_PTR (MSR 0x17D0) and set the valid bit
3. SW will check CPUID.6.EDX[31:16] on each logical processor to get it's index into the HFI table
4. SW will clear IA32_PACKAGE_THERM_STATUS[HW_FEEDBACK] 
5. SW can choose to enable the IA32_PACKAGE_THERM_INTERRUPT[HW_FEEDBACK] bit to receive thermal interrupts when the HFI table is updated
6. Lastly, SW will write the enable bit in IA32_HW_FEEDBACK_CONFIG MSR to enable HGS reporting

functions.name: disable_hgs
functions.doc:
Disables HGS
1. SW should clear IA32_PACKAGE_THERM_STATUS[HW_FEEDBACK] and then clear IA32_HW_FEEDBACK_CONFIG to signal that it is ready to disable HGS.
2. Pcode should detect that the HW_FEEDBACK bit fell from 1->0 and no longer update the HFI table.
3. Software will wait for one final setting of IA32_PACKAGE_THERM_STATUS[HW_FEEDBACK] to know that pcode is no longer using the memory region
4. After the final setting of THERM_STATUS[HW_FEEDBACK], SW can free the allocated memory with no ill system behavior observed

module_doc:
Designs and creates a custom logger.
Originally made for SST scripts, but general enough it can be used in other domains.

Functions:
    _create_formatter,
    add_custom_handler,
    create_custom_logger,
    create_custom_logger_with_handler
Classes:
    custom_logger
Main in this file

classes.name: custom_logger
classes.doc:
Custom logger class for scripting. Initialize with create_custom_logger() function.

Inherited from logging.Logger class. Adds additional log levels to the standard 5:
    debug < sample (new) < info < warning < error < result (new) < critical < header (new)

Args:
    logger_name = (str) Name of the logger. Recommend to be related to the module the logger runs in.

Unique methods:
    sample - Log a message at level sample
    result - Log a message at level result
    header - Log a message at level header

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc:
Init for custom_logger

Args:
    logger_name = (str) Name of the logger. Recommend to be related to the module the logger runs in.

methods.name: sample
methods.doc: Send a log message at level SAMPLE=15

methods.name: result
methods.doc: Send a log message at level RESULT=45

methods.name: header
methods.doc: Send a log message at level HEADER=55

functions.name: _create_formatter
functions.doc:
Creates a formatter for the handlers of custom_logger class.

There are two formats. One with the name.
    13:35:36 logger_name   HEADER : Message
One without the name.
    13:35:36   HEADER : Message
Since the date is omitted for brevity, consider including the date in the header/first few lines of the log.

Args:
    include_name = (bool, optional) If the formatter should include the name. Default=True.

Returns:
    (obj) A logging.Formatter instance

functions.name: add_custom_handler
functions.doc:
Creates a file handler for the custom logger.

Args:
    - base_logger = (logger) The logger the handler should be added to
    - file_name = (str) Path and file name the handler writes to
    - log_level = (int or str) The logging level of the handler
    - include_name = (bool, optional) If the name of the logger should be included in messages

Returns:
    No return value

functions.name: create_custom_logger
functions.doc:
Creates custom logger and removes default handler to it. Will add own stdout/console handler

Args:
    - logger_name = (str) Name of the logger. Recommend to be related to the module the logger runs in.
    - console_level = (int or str, optional) Logging level for console prints. Default=warning
    - include_name = (bool, optional) If logger name should be included for console prints. Default=True.

Returns:
    (obj) custom_logger instance

functions.name: create_custom_logger_with_handler
functions.doc:
Creates custom logger and applies particular handlers to the logger:
    - a file handler that writes to a .log file at level log_level
    - a stdout handler that writes at level console_level

Args:
    - logger_name = (str) Name of the logger. Recommend to be related to the module the logger runs in.
    - file_name = (str) Name of the file to store all logs to. Must be a .log file.
    - log_level = (str or int, optional) Level of the log file. Default='sample'.
    - console_level = (str or int, optional) Level to output to console. Default='error'.
    - include_name_con = (bool, optional) If console prints should include the logger's name. Default=True.
    - include_name_log = (bool, optional) If the log file should include the logger's name. Default=False.

Returns:
    (obj) custom_logger instance

functions.name: main
functions.doc:
An example script that shows the intended usage of all levels for the custom_logger class.

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    None

module_doc:
Module for feature-specific utils.

Classes:
    pp
    cp
    bf
    tf
Main in this file

classes.name: pp
classes.doc:
All utility functions for SST-PP.

Methods (all methods are static):
    - _set_pp_level_on_die      :use TPMI to change the PP level on a die
    - set_pp_level              :change the PP level on a socket
    - get_pp_level              :get the PP level on a socket
    - get_pp_lock               :get the PP lock status
    - get_active_cores          :get which cores are active at current PP level
    - _get_trl_bucket           :get which TRL bucket the cbb die is in
    - get_pp_info_tpmi          :get all PP information from TPMI registers
    - get_trl_avg_from_cores    :get the average TRL by reading wp4 targets
    - determine_turbo_freq      :get the TRL for the cbb die
    - determine_highest_trl     :get the highest TRL, regardless of core count
    - get_available_levels      :get the available PP levels on a socket
    - get_error                 :get the error message on the status register on TPMI
    - get_avx_freq              :get the P1 frequency of each instruction type
    - enable_hgs                :enable HGS
    - determine_resolved_mask   :determine what the resolved mask should be per PP level
    - determine_core_enabled    :using sst_pp_info_2, determine which cores should be enabled

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc: The class should not have to be instantiated because it is just a holder for methods.

methods.name: _set_pp_level_on_die
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to write to register sst_pp_control of the specified socket+die to change PP level, but leave BF and TF at the same state.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) Name of the socket to assign data to.
    - die_name      = (str) Name of the die of the specified socket.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) PP-level you want this component to go to. Must be a valid PP level. Default=0.
    - oob           = (bool, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use OOB to write to the registers. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise 0.

methods.name: set_pp_level
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to write to register sst_pp_control to all dies of the specified socket to change PP level, but leave BF and
TF at the same state.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to change level. Default=socket0
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) PP-level you want this component to go to. Must be in _VALID_PP_LEVEL. Default=0.
    - force_level   = (bool, optional) If PP level should be asserted regardless of capability. Default=False.
    - oob           = (bool, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use OOB to write to the registers. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors preventing level change, otherwise 0.

methods.name: get_pp_level
methods.doc:
Gets the PP-level of the specified socket+die.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the PP-level. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise, the PP-level, in _VALID_PP_LEVEL.

methods.name: get_pp_lock
methods.doc:
Gets the lock status of the specified socket+die.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the lock status. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise 1 if locked, 0 if unlocked

methods.name: get_active_cores
methods.doc:
Will see which cores are active on the specified socket+die. Reads register ucode_cr_perf_status of each core to
determine if the core is active. Will then create a mask with a 1 if the core is active, 0 if inactive. Bit[i] corresponds
to the status of core_i.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get active core mask from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise the active core mask, with a 1 in bit[i] if core_i is active

methods.name: _get_trl_bucket
methods.doc:
Will get which TRL bucket a specified socket+die is in. Will read TPMI reg sst_pp_info_10 of the specified
socket+die. This reg gives the ranges of each bucket. Will then get the component's number of cores and current
PP-level. Will then use that info to look at the ranges and determine what bucket the die is currently in.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get buckets from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die from the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise the bucket the specified number of cores is in.

methods.name: get_pp_info_tpmi
methods.doc:
Via TPMI, get the SST-PP info register values and parse to get relevant information. See return value for what
information the method will get.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get active core mask from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) PP-level to get info on. If not in [range(-1, 5)], then will use level=0.If
                        Default=-1, will use the component's current PP-level. 

Return: (dict) Will return an empty dict if there are any errors, otherwise
    - Key                   | Value
    - tdp                   | (int) TDP for this level, in units of one-eighth-Watt.
    - mem_freq              | (int) Maximum freq of memory, in units of MHz.
    - tprochot              | (int) TProchot/TJmax of the level.
    - module_enable_mask    | (int) Mask of which modules are enabled.
    - core_f_ratio_p0       | (int) Core maximum freq ratio
    - core_f_ratio_p1       | (int) Core TDP freq ratio
    - core_f_ratio_pn       | (int) Core max-efficiency freq ratio
    - core_f_ratio_pm       | (int) Core minimum freq ratio
    - uncore_f_ratio_p0     | (int) Uncore maximum freq ratio
    - uncore_f_ratio_p1     | (int) Uncore TDP freq ratio
    - uncore_f_ratio_pm     | (int) Uncore minimum freq ratio
    - p1_f_ratio_license_0  | (int) P1 freq ratio of SSE (aka base/ICCP license level 1).
    - p1_f_ratio_license_1  | (int) P1 freq ratio of AVX2 (ICCP license level 2).
    - p1_f_ratio_license_2  | (int) P1 freq ratio of AVX512 (ICCP license level 3).
    - p1_f_ratio_license_3  | (int) P1 freq ratio of AMX (ICCP license level 4).
    - trl_license_{0 to 3}  | (int) TRL for this license of the die's bucket.

methods.name: get_trl_avg_from_cores
methods.doc:
Get the average TRL from the cores of the socket+die by reading wp4 targets.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the info from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die from the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns: (dict) CLOS/license ranges from 0 to 3
    - Key                   | Value
    - {int of clos group}   | (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise average TRL for this license level.

methods.name: determine_turbo_freq
methods.doc:
Will read PP TPMI regs to determine the target turbo ratio limit TRL of the specified socket+die.
- Determine what PP level the device is in
- Use TPMI on the specified socket+die to read registers sst_pp_info_# and build the reference tables
- Determine what PP bucket the device is in depending on the number of cores it has
- Use that information against the table and then return the turbo ratios for each cdyn license

Note that this only gets the TRL for PP. TF TRL may be observed if TF is enabled.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get TRL. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) The PP level of the specified component. If Default=-1, will automatically fetch.

Returns: (dict) The TRLs for all CDYN licences. Will be {} if there are any errors.
    - Key               | Value
    - {int of license}  | (int) Turbo ratio for this license

methods.name: determine_highest_trl
methods.doc:
Will read all info TPMI registers of the specified socket+die+pplevel and determine what is the highest turbo ratio limit.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read the TPMI registers. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the specified die on the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) The PP level of the info registers to read from. If Default=-1, will automatically get PP level.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any errors, otherwise the highest TRL

methods.name: get_available_levels
methods.doc:
Will read TPMI reg SST_PP_HEADER from specified socket+die to get the available PP levels for dynamic switching. Note that although there
may be multiple levels available, SST_PP_STATUS.SST_PP_LOCK will always determine if PP levels can be changed.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get info on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (list of int) The levels that are available to switch to

methods.name: get_error
methods.doc:
Will read TPMI reg SST_PP_STATUS to get errors from the last control

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read status reg. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (list of int) The errors of each feature [pp_error, bf_error, tf_error]. A 0 means there was no error and a 1 means there was an error.
    -1 means that the function failed to read the register.

methods.name: get_avx_freq
methods.doc:
Will get the base/P1 frequency at all avx workloads at the specified PP-level.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) The name of the socket to get frequencies. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) The name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) The PP level of what frequencies to get. If Default=-1, will automatically get PP-level.

Returns: (dict) P1 frequencies of avx workloads. Value will be -1 if there is any error.
    - Key       | Value
    - sse       | (int) P1 of sse instructions
    - avx2      | (int) P1 of avx2 instructions
    - avx512    | (int) P1 of avx512 instructions
    - amx       | (int) P1 of amx instructions

methods.name: enable_hgs
methods.doc:
Enables HGS on the specified socket. Will do nothing if HGS is already enabled.

Args:
    socket_name = (str, optional) The name of the socket to enable HGS on. Default=socket0.

Returns:
    (int) 1 if there is any error, otherwise 0

methods.name: determine_resolved_mask
methods.doc:
From the SST-PP mask disable fuses (which is a physical mask), determine the resolved mask for each PP level which is written in
sst_pp_info_2 (which is a logical mask).

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read fuses from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - boot_pp_level = (int, optional) The PP level that the device booted with. If Default=-1, will automatically get boot PP level.
    - map_obj       = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns:
    (list of int) The resolved masks of each PP level: return_val[PP level] = mask. For levels lower than the boot level,
    will use the mask of the boot level. Depending on usage, you may need to convert to hex.

methods.name: determine_core_enabled
methods.doc:
Will check the core mask in sst_pp_info_2 to see which cores are enabled

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to check. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - map_obj       = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns: (dict) Which cores are enabled.
    - Key               | Value
    - {int of core PID} | (bool) If the core is enabled

classes.name: cp
classes.doc:
All utility functions for SST-CP.

Methods (all methods are static):
    - _enable_cp_on_die         :use TPMI to enable CP on a die
    - enable_cp                 :enable CP or change priority type on a socket
    - get_cp_status             :see if CP is enabled and what is the priority type on a socket
    - get_cp_error              :get the current error on the CP status register in TPMI
    - assign_priority           :change the priority type on a socket
    - get_clos_ids              :get the CLOS groups of all cores on a cbb die
    - _get_clos_ids_from_all    :get the CLOS groups of all cores on a socket
    - assign_clos_ids           :assign the CLOS IDs of all cores on a cbb die
    - assign_all_clos_ids       :assign the CLOS IDs of all cores on a cbb die the same across a socket
    - assign_random_clos_ids    :assign random CLOS IDs
    - assign_even_clos_ids      :assign an even amount of cores to all CLOS groups
    - _count_clos_on_die        :get the number of cores in each CLOS group on a cbb die
    - count_cores_per_clos      :get the number of cores in each CLOS group on a socket
    - get_clos_cfg              :get the config of a CLOS group
    - get_all_clos_cfg          :get the config of all CLOS groups
    - assign_clos_cfg           :assign the config of a CLOS group
    - assign_all_cfg            :assign the config for all CLOS groups
    - get_hp_count              :get the number of cores in CLOS0 (high priority)
    - assign_random_clos_cfg    :randomly assign values to CLOS cfg and CLOS assoc on a socket
    - _prio_flow                :do the prioritization flow for SST-CP to get the core targets
    - get_excursion             :get the excursion to min on a die
    - assign_hwp_per_thread     :give HWP hints to the cores/threads
    - assign_hwp_per_pkg        :give HWP hint to a socket

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc: The class should not have to be instantiated because it is just a holder for methods.

methods.name: _enable_cp_on_die
methods.doc:
Will enable (or disable) SST-CP on the specified socket+die. The specified die must be CP capable, else this function cannot enable CP.
Use this function to also disable CP with disable=True.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to enable CP. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket to enable. Default=imh0.
    - priority_type     = (str, optional) The CLOS priority type when enabling this die. One of [ordered, proportional]. Default='ordered'.
    - skip_capability   = (bool, optional) If the function should check if the die is CP capable before attempting to enable CP.
                            Default=False.
    - disable_cp        = (bool, optional) If the function should disable CP instead of enable. Default=False.
    - tpmi_access       = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, 0 otherwise

methods.name: enable_cp
methods.doc:
Will enable (or disable) SST-CP on the specified socket. The specified socket must be CP capable, else this function will
not enable CP (override with skip_cap). Also use to set priority type.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to enable CP. Default=socket0.
    - priority_type     = (str, optional) The CLOS priority type when enabling this die. One of [ordered, proportional]. Default='ordered'.
    - skip_cap          = (bool, optional) If the function should check if CP capable before attempting to enable CP. Default=False.
    - disable_cp        = (bool, optional) If the function should disable CP instead of enable. Default=False.
    - tpmi_access       = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Returns:
    (int) The number of errors, 0 otherwise

methods.name: get_cp_status
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to read the SST_CP_STATUS register of the specified socket+die.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read register from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - tpmi_access   = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Returns: (tuple)
    - (bool) If CP is enabled,
    - (str) Priority type; either [proportional, ordered, error]; will be error if there was an error with TPMI

methods.name: get_cp_error
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to read the SST_CP_STATUS register of the specified socket+die to get the error

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read register from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - tpmi_access   = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if TPMI could not be used, 1 if there was an error, otherwise 0

methods.name: assign_priority
methods.doc:
Will set priority type on specified socket without changing if CP is enabled or not.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to enable CP. Default=socket0.
    - priority_type = (str, optional) The CLOS priority type when enabling this die. One of [ordered,proportional]. Default='ordered'.
    - oob           = (bool, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use OOB to write to the registers. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, 0 otherwise

methods.name: get_clos_ids
methods.doc:
Reads the CLOS association registers to get the IDs of each core. Note that these registers are by logical ID. Will
automatically map to physical ID; see arg use_pid for more info.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get ids from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die from the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - use_pid       = (bool, optional) If True, the return value should use the core's PID instead of LID. Default=True.
    - map_obj       = (coreMap object, needed only if use_pid=True) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically
                        get a mapping.
    - tpmi_access   = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.
    - log           = (logging.Logger, optional) Logger object for logging. Default=_log.

Returns:
    - (dict) The CLOS ID of each core. The core num will be (PID if use_pid else LID). Will be {} if there is an error.
        - Key               | Value
        - {int of core num} | (int) CLOS ID of that core.

methods.name: _get_clos_ids_from_all
methods.doc:
For debug, to print the CLOS IDs for all cbb dies.

Args:
    socket_name = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get IDs from. Default=socket0.

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: assign_clos_ids
methods.doc:
Will assign CLOS IDs using TPMI writes to SST_CLOS_ASSOC registers. Note that this register is for the physical
enumeration PID not logical LID. By default the function assumes you are using LID; see use_pid arg for more info.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket you wish to assign CLOS IDs. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - assignment_dict   = (dict, optional) Which IDs are assigned to what core. If Default={}, will change the IDs of all cores to default_id.
        - Keys              | Value
        - {int of core num} | (int) CLOS ID of this core. One of [range(4)].
    - default_id        = (int, optional) For cores not specified in assignment_dict, the ID that it will be assigned. Default=0.
    - use_pid           = (bool, optional) If assignment_dict should interpret the core enumeration as PID not LID. Default=False.
    - map_obj           = (coreMap object, needed only if use_pid=True) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get
                            a mapping.
    - tpmi_access       = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, 0 otherwise.

methods.name: assign_all_clos_ids
methods.doc:
Will use function assign_clos_ids on all cbbs of the specified die. All cbbs will have the same assignment of CLOS groups by
logical ID.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket you wish to assign CLOS IDs. Default=socket0.
    - assignment_dict   = (dict, optional) Which IDs are assigned to what core. If Default={}, will assign all cores to default_id.
        - Keys              | Value
        - {int of core num} | (int) CLOS ID of this core. One of [range(4)].
    - default_id        = (int, optional) For cores not specified in assignment_dict, the ID that it will be assigned. Default=0.
    - use_pid           = (bool, optional) If assignment_dict should interpret the core enumeration as PID not LID. Default=False.
    - map_obj           = (coreMap object, needed only if use_pid=True) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get
                            a mapping.
    - oob               = (bool, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use OOB to write to the registers. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) 0 if there are no errors, otherwise the negative of the count of cbbs that could not be assigned

methods.name: assign_random_clos_ids
methods.doc:
Will use function assign_clos_ids to assign random CLOS IDs over all cores on the specified socket+die.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to assign IDs. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. If Default='all', will assign random IDs on all dies.
    - tpmi_access   = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise 0

methods.name: assign_even_clos_ids
methods.doc:
Will use function assign_clos_ids to assign CLOS IDs such that there are an even number of cores in each CLOS group.

Args:
    - socket_name = (str, optional) Name of the socket to assign IDs. Default=socket0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise 0

methods.name: _count_clos_on_die
methods.doc:
On specified socket+die, will look at the ID of each core and return the count of cores in each CLOS group.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to look at IDs. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns: (list of int) Count of each group
    - return_val[int of CLOS group] = number of cores in that group

methods.name: count_cores_per_clos
methods.doc:
On specified socket, will look at the ID of each core on each die and return the count of cores in each CLOS group.

Args:
    socket_name = (str, optional) Name of the socket to look at IDs. Default=socket0.

Returns: (list of int) Count of each group
    - return_val[int of CLOS group] = number of cores in that group

methods.name: get_clos_cfg
methods.doc:
Will use tpmi_read for SST_CLOS_CONFIG_# register of the specified socket+die. Note that the config on the root die (imh0)
is what must be programmed for SST-CP to work correctly. The cfg_num corresponds to the config of that CLOS group.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read the register from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. In almost all cases you should be reading from
                        Default=imh0 which is root die.
    - cfg_num       = (int, optional) The number of the config reg to read from. Must be in [range(4)]. Default=0.
    - tpmi_access   = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Returns: (dict) The data from the register. Will be {} if there are any errors
    - Key       | Value
    - min_f     | (int) The minimum freq for this CLOS group, in units of 100Mhz.
    - max_f     | (int) The maximum freq for this CLOS group, in units of 100Mhz.
    - priority  | (int) The priority of this CLOS group in proportional mode. Within [range(16)].

methods.name: get_all_clos_cfg
methods.doc:
Will apply get_clos_cfg on all CLOS IDs of root die (imh0) and combine into one dict.

Args:
    - socket_name = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get CLOS config info from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name    = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - tpmi_access = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Returns: (dict) All CLOS config information
    - Key           | Value
    - {int of ID}   | (dict) return val of get_clos_cfg
        - Key       | Value
        - min_f     | (int) The minimum freq for this CLOS group, in units of 100Mhz.
        - max_f     | (int) The maximum freq for this CLOS group, in units of 100Mhz.
        - priority  | (int) The priority of this CLOS group in proportional mode. Within [range(16)].

methods.name: assign_clos_cfg
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to write to sst_clos_config_# register. Defines the limits/priority of the CLOS group, specified by cfg_num. Does these
writes only on root die (imh0), which is where the RAPL algorithm consumes the SST-CP parameters.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do assignments. Default=socket0.
