    - cfg_num       = (int, optional) The number of the config register to assign to. One of [range(4)]. Default=0.
    - min_f         = (int, optional) Minimum freq ratio for this CLOS group, in units of 100MHz. Must be less than max_f. Within
                        range(256)/0x00-0xFF. Default=0.
    - max_f         = (int, optional) Maximum freq ratio for this CLOS group, in units of 100MHz. Within range(256)/0x00-0xFF. Default=255.
    - priority      = (int, optional) Priority if CLOS is in proportional mode. Within range(16)/0x0-0xF. Default=0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) The die to assign config. Should be Default=imh0 which is root die.
    - tpmi_access   = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Return:
    -1 if there is any errors, 0 otherwise.

methods.name: assign_all_cfg
methods.doc:
Will do assign_clos_cfg on all CLOS groups. List arguments must all be long enough for all CLOS groups.

Args:
    - min_list      = (list of int) The minimum frequencies to set. CLOS_n.min = min_list[n]
    - max_list      = (list of int) The maximum frequencies to set. CLOS_n.max = max_list[n]
    - pri_list      = (list of int) The priority to set. CLOS_n.proportional_priority = pri_list[n]
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do assignments. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) The die to assign config. Should be Default=imh0 which is root die.
    - tpmi_access   = (str, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use Inband or OOB to write to the registers. Choices = [oob, inband, mailbox]. Default=mailbox.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise 0

methods.name: get_hp_count
methods.doc:
Will use get_clos_ids() to get the CLOS for each core. Counts the number of cores that are are CLOS0 and CLOS1 (high
priortiy) and will return the total.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to count HP cores. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise the number of HP cores

methods.name: assign_random_clos_cfg
methods.doc:
Will do random configuration for CLOS/CP for specified socket+die.

For CLOS cfg, will give each group a random min and max within the socket's P0 and Pm, and random priority in range(0, 16). For CLOS assoc,
will give each core a random CLOS ID. Config changes will be written to imh0 regardless of die_name, whereas die_name is used for CLOS assoc.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do random assignments. Default='socket0'.
    - same_min      = (str, optional) If all groups should have the same min freq. Default=False.
    - same_max      = (str, optional) If all groups should have the same max freq. Default=False.
    - same_priority = (str, optional) If all groups should have the same priority. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise 0

methods.name: _prio_flow
methods.doc:
A psuedo function that does the prioritization flow of SST-CP to determine what is the granted frequency of each CLOS group.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to simulate the flow. Default=socket0.
    - mode          = (int, optional) 0 if proportional mode, 1 if ordered mode. If Default=-1, will automatically get what priority mode is
                        on the socket.

Returns:
    (list of int) return_val[int of CLOS group] = The granted frequency of that CLOS group

methods.name: get_excursion
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to read reg SST_CP_STATUS to get EXCURSION_TO_MIN of the specified socket+die.

SW is expected to reset/clear the excursion bits when using these values. This is done by writing
0 to reg SST_CP_CONTROL.RESET_EXCURSION_TO_MIN.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read reg. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - clear_excursion   = (bool, optional) If to also clear excursion bits. Default=False.

Returns:
    (list of int) Will be [] if there is any errors. Otherwise, list[CLOS #] is 1 if that CLOS group experienced an excursion to min
    from the previous control. Length depends on _VALID_CLOS_IDS (4).

methods.name: assign_hwp_per_thread
methods.doc:
Will give hints to HWP to various cores.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to assign HWP hints to. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - assignment_list   = (list of int) What value to give each thread. list[0] will write to core0.thread0, list[1] will write to
                            core0.thread1, list[2] will write to core1.thread0, etc.
    - hint_type          = (str, optional) What type of hint to write to for each of the threads. One of [min, max, des]. Default=min.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise 0

methods.name: assign_hwp_per_pkg
methods.doc:
Write to MSR hwp_request_pkg to change performance hints. Note that only threads that have
pcu_cr_ia32_hwp_request.package_control set to 1 will honor these parameters.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to assign HWP hints to. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - min_perf      = (int, optional) The minimum performance hint. If Default=-1, leaves unchanged.
    - max_perf      = (int, optional) The maximum performance hint. If Default=-1, leaves unchanged.
    - des_perf      = (int, optional) The target/desired performance hint. If Default=-1, leaves unchanged.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise 0

classes.name: bf
classes.doc:
All utility functions for SST-BF.

Methods (all methods are static):
    - get_bf_status                 :get if BF is enabled
    - get_bf_info_tpmi              :get all the BF info in TPMI registers
    - get_hp_core_mask              :get the core mask for BF HP cores by physical IDs
    - get_p1_freq                   :get the P1 core frequencies for HP and LP cores
    - _is_bf_hp                     :by using the logical to physical core map get the cores which are are BF HP
    - enable_bf                     :enable BF via TPMI
    - determine_resolved_hp_mask    :by using the logical to physical core map get the resolved HP mask

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc: The class should not have to be instantiated because it is just a holder for methods.

methods.name: get_bf_status
methods.doc:
Uses TPMI on SST_PP_STATUS on specified socket+die to see if BF is currently enabled.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to check if BF is enabled. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket to get status. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise 1 if BF is enabled, 0 if disabled.

methods.name: get_bf_info_tpmi
methods.doc:
Via TPMI, read from the SST-BF info registers of the specified socket+die of the specified PP level.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read TPMI from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) Which PP level to read info registers. If Default=-1, will automatically get the
                        PP level of the die.

Return: (dict) Will be {} if any errors, otherwise
    - Key       | Value
    - tdp       | (int) TDP when BF is enabled
    - p1_hi     | (int) Base frequency P1 for high priority cores
    - p1_lo     | (int) Base frequency P1 for low priority cores
    - mask      | (int) Core mask for high priority cores. Bit_n == 1 means core_n is high priority
    - t_control | (int) Temperature fan offset
    - t_prochot | (int) Prochot/maximum tmeperature

methods.name: get_hp_core_mask
methods.doc:
Will read the mask of which cores are high priority HP for the specified socket+die. Gets this information through TPMI
reg SST_BF_INFO_1. This reg has width _MASK_WIDTH (64), so the return list with have length 64. Note that this is logical
core ID LID.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get mask from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) The PP level to get mask from. If Default=-1, will use the current PP level of the
                        component.

Returns:
    (list of int) [] if there are any errors. Otherwise, list[core LID] is 1 if the core is HP, 0 otherwise.

methods.name: get_p1_freq
methods.doc:
Reads TPMI reg SST_BF_INFO_0 of the specified socket+die and gets the base freq of high priority
HP and low priority LP cores.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read TPMI reg. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.

Returns: (dict) Will be {} if any errors. Otherwise, the base/P1 frequency in 100Mhz
    - Key   | Value
    - hi    | (int) Frequency for HP cores.
    - lo    | (int) Frequency for LP cores.

methods.name: _is_bf_hp
methods.doc:
Will read the high priority mask in TPMI sst_bf_info_1 to get which cores are considered high priority.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read mask. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - map_obj       = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns: (dict) Which cores are high priority
    - Key               | Value
    - {int of core pid} | (int) 1 if the core is HP, 0 if the core is LP, -1 if the core is disabled

methods.name: enable_bf
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to write to SST_PP_CONTROL to enable/disable BF.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to enable BF. Default=socket0.
    - bf_en             = (bool, optional) If BF should be enabled on this component, otherwise BF will be disabled. Default=True.
    - skip_capability   = (bool, optional) If the capability check should be skipped. Default=False.
    - spoof_driver      = (bool, optional) If CLOS config should be set, spoofing the SST driver. Default=True.
    - oob               = (bool, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use OOB to write to the registers. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise 0.

methods.name: determine_resolved_hp_mask
methods.doc:
Will read the fuse pcode_sst_bf_config_#_p1_hi_core_mask and use the PID to LID mapping to determine what the high priority
mask should be. This goal is to see if TPMI register sst_bf_info_1 is populated with the correct value.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read mask. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - map_obj       = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns: (list of int)
    return_val[int of pp level] = resolved HP mask

classes.name: tf
classes.doc:
All utility functions for SST-TF.

Methods (all methods are static):
    - get_tf_status             :see if TF is enabled
    - _build_tf_bucket_bins     :create the bins for TF TRL
    - get_trl_bucket            :by looking at the HP cores of a cbb die, determine what TRL bucket the die is in
    - determine_turbo_freq_hp   :determine the turbo frequency for high priority cores
    - determine_turbo_freq_lp   :determine the turbo frequency for low priority cores
    - get_legacy_fused_trl      :the fused legacy turbo frequency used when bins are 0
    - get_legacy_reg_trl        :the MSR legacy turbo frequency used when bins are 0
    - enable_tf                 :enable TF on a socket

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc: The class should not have to be instantiated because it is just a holder for methods.

methods.name: get_tf_status
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to read SST_PP_STATUS to see if TF is currently enabled.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of socket to get TF status from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of die of the specified socket to read. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise 1 if TF is enabled, 0 if disabled.

methods.name: _build_tf_bucket_bins
methods.doc:
Using TPMI register sst_tf_info_1, creates the bins for each TF bucket.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read the register from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) The PP for which info register to read. If Default=-1, will automatically get the PP level
                        of the die.

Returns: (dict) The bounds of each bucket. {} if there is any error
    - Key                   | Value
    - {int of the bucket}   | (tuple) (int) Lowest number of cores for this bucket, (int) Highest number

methods.name: get_trl_bucket_on_die
methods.doc:
Will get which TRL bucket a specified die is in. Will read TPMI reg sst_tf_info_1 of the specified socket+die. This reg gives the
ranges of each bucket. Will then get the component's number of HP cores and current PP-level. Will then use that info to look at the
ranges and determine what bucket the die is currently in.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get buckets from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level      = (int, int) The PP-level of the die. If Default=-1, will automatically get PP-level.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise the bucket the specified number of cores is in.

methods.name: determine_turbo_freq_hp
methods.doc:
Will get the high priority HP turbo ratios of each cdyn/licence level determined by the number of high priority (HP) cores.
- Determine what PP level the device is in
- Use TPMI on the specified socket to read registers sst_tf_info_# and build the reference tables
- Look at the CLOS IDs of cores to get the number of high priority cores on each cbb
- Determine what TF bucket each cbb is in depending on the number of HP cores
- Use that information against the table and get the turbo ratios for each cdyn/license level
- Get the max from all cbbs

If the number of HP cores is greater than the largest TF bucket, the device is to use its legacy fused value (the worst
case value assuming ALL cores get the same turbo frequency).

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) The name of the socket to read TPMI regs from. Default=socket0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) PP-level of the specified die. If Default=-1, will automatically get PP-level

Returns: (tuple)
    - (bool) True if the using the legacy value since the device doesn't fall into any bucket
    - (dict) The TRLs for all cdyn levels. Will be {} if there are any errors
       - Key                | Value
       - {int of license}   | (int) Turbo ratio for this license

methods.name: determine_turbo_freq_lp
methods.doc:
Will get the low priority LP turbo ratios of each cdyn/licence level.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) The name of the socket to read TPMI regs from. Default=socket0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) PP-level of the specified die. If Default=-1, will automatically get PP-level.

Returns: (dict) The TRLs for all cdyn levels
    - Key               | Value
    - {int of license}  | (int) Turbo ratio for this level

methods.name: get_legacy_fused_trl
methods.doc:
Will get the legacy/default TRL for the specified socket+die. Will read fuse base.fuses.punit_fuses.fw_fuses_ia_p0_ratio. This value is what
used when SST-TF is disabled or if the devices has more cores than the largest TF bucket.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read the fuse from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) The PP-level of the die. Default=0.
    - tf_bucket     = (int, optional) The bucket the die is in. If Default=None, will return value for all buckets.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise the value of the legacy TRL

methods.name: get_legacy_reg_trl
methods.doc:
Will read reg turbo_ratio_limit_cores to determine the number of cores for each bucket, then read reg
turbo_ratio_limit_ratios to get the legacy turbo frequency for that bucket. In case the register is not properly populated,
will return the fused TRL as a fallback value.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of socket to read the reg from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - num_core      = (int, optional) The number of cores in the specified die. If Default=-1, will automatically get the number
                        of cores.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise the TRL of the die

methods.name: random_clos_for_tf
methods.doc:
Will randomly assign CLOS IDs to the cores for testing. Will ensure that BF HP cores will be TF HP cores.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to change CLOS IDs. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die to change CLOS IDs. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise 0

methods.name: enable_tf
methods.doc:
Will use TPMI to write to SST_PP_CONTROL to enable/disable TF.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to enable TF. Default=socket0.
    - tf_en             = (bool, optional) If TF should be enabled on this component, otherwise TF will be disabled. Default=True.
    - skip_capability   = (bool, optional) If the capability check should be skipped. Default=False.
    - spoof_driver      = (bool, optional) If CLOS config should be set, spoofing the SST driver. Default=False.
    - hp_cores          = (int, optional) The number of cores to be set as high priority. Will do nothing unless arg spoof_driver
                            is set to True. Default=1.
    - oob               = (bool, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use OOB to write to the registers.
                            Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if specified socket is TF incapable, otherwise the number of failed assignments.

module_doc:
Utility functions and main testing class for SST in DMR.

Functions:
    === MISC UTILS ===
    parse_die_name,
    apply_mask,
    remove_mask,
    build_bins,
    _debug_target_info,
    time_response,
    _iterate_on_path,

    === TPMI UTILS ===
    _get_field_bits,
    _get_access_of_reg,
    _get_field_from_reg,
    build_dict_from_read,
    build_default_dict,
    build_value_for_write,
    tpmi_read,
    tpmi_write,

    === OOB UTILS ===
    _fill_in_mask,
    _fit_field_in_value,
    write_to_tpmi_via_oob,

    === SST UTILS ===
    are_fuses_loaded,
    get_valid_pp_levels,
    get_lowest_pp_level,
    get_boot_pp_level,
    get_capability,
    get_all_sst_regs,
    get_reg_from_all_dies,
    get_core_list,
    get_num_cores,
    check_hwp_interrupt,
    force_turbo_enable,
    _get_turbo_status,
    print_info_table,
    _set_pp_ctl_on_die,
    _get_assignment_list,
    set_pp_ctl,
    is_turbo_enabled,

    === SCRIPT UTILS ===
    _create_sst_logger,
    compare_info_dict

Classes:
    coreMap,
    validateSS

classes.name: coreMap
classes.doc:
Class holding functions to create the core map from physical IDs to logical IDs of the cores.

Args:
    No args

Methods:
    - _lli_from_core            :(static) Get the local logical inst from core.target_info
    - _ldi_from_pmsb            :(static) Get the logical die id from core_pmsb.target_info
    - _search_pmsb              :(static) Search all core_pmsb to see if one has a LID that matches
    - build_core_map            :build a mapping from physical ID to logical ID
    - build_core_map_reversed   :build a mapping from logical ID to physical ID
    - print_core_map            :print the core mapping, to help with debug
    - cores_on_die              :gt all cores on a compute by PID or LID, by access method

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc:
Init for coreMap class.

Args:
    No args

Attributes:
    - mapmethod (str) = Current map method. One of [target, host, stub].

methods.name: _lli_from_core
methods.doc:
From the compute space, get a core's local logical inst.

Args:
    - core_num      = (int) The core LID from the cpu.cores enumeration
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket the core belongs to. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the compute die on the specified the socket the core belongs to. Default=compute0.

Returns:
    (int) The core ID of the specified core

methods.name: _ldi_from_pmsb
methods.doc:
From uncore.core_pmsb space, get a pmsb's logical die id.

If the pmsb has key 'active_core' == False, will return -1

Args:
    - sb_num        = (int) The pmsb ID from the uncore.core_pmsb enumeration
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get mapping. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=compute0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if the pmsb does not exist or the is not active, else the logical_die_id of the pmsb

methods.name: _search_pmsb
methods.doc:
Looking at all pmsbs, will attempt to find one whose logical die id matches the specified value.

Args:
    - module_num      = (int) The ID that should be looked for
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get mapping. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if no match is found, else the pmsb that had a match

methods.name: build_core_map
methods.doc:
Creates a dict map from physical ID to logical ID. Note that all -1 if in stubmode since the ID will not exist.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to core IDs from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns: (dict) Mapping of IDs
    - Key       | Value
    - {int PID} | (int) -1 if physical core is inactive, otherwise the core's LID

methods.name: build_core_map_reversed
methods.doc:
Creates a dict map from logical ID to physical ID. This is the reverse mapping from the _build_core_map funciton. Note
that the dict will be empty when in stubmode since the IDs do not exist.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to core IDs from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns: (dict) Mapping of IDs
    - Key       | Value
    - {int LID} | (int) The core's PID

methods.name: print_core_map
methods.doc:
Visual map of all cores to match LID to PID

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get cores from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: cores_on_die
methods.doc:
Creates a dict map from the cores on die-scope to the LID and PID.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to core IDs from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns: (dict) Mapping of IDs
    - Key                           | Value
    - {str die-scoped core inst}    | (tuple of int) The core's PID, the core's LID

classes.name: validateSS
classes.doc:
Class for validating various SST functionalities.

Example usage of instantiation if running from this module in main:
    - opt_parser = validateSS.get_options()
    - opt_dict = opt_parser.parse_args('-l debug --stubmode'.split())
    - val_SS = validateSS(opt_dict)

Args:
    - option_dict   = (dict) Option dict from instantiated parser object from validateSS.get_options method.
    - sst_feature   = (str) The SST feature for this object. One of [bf, cp, pp, tf].
    - attr_dict     = (dict) What to populate attribute self.component_dict. Must have at minimum
                        {'mismatch_list': []} so that _compare_fuses method works properly.
        - Keys  | Value
        - {key} | (any) Default value for this key.
    - with_handler  = (bool, optional) If the logger should automatically include the log/file handler. If False, the user
                        should be the one to add the file handler. Default=True.
    - script_path   = (path obj, optional) The path of the script that initializes the instance. This is to help logging keep
                        track of which script is executing when running multiple. Default=None.

Methods:
    - get_options               :(static) Arg parser for command line options
    - add_additional_handler    :add a file handler for logger, to be used if one is not provided on object init
    - iterate_over_components   :have a function iterate over all dies on all sockets
    - iterate_over_sockets      :have a function iterate over all sockets
    - get_all_capability        :iterate function get_capability() on all dies, updating attripute component_dict

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc:
Init for validateSS class.

Args:
    - option_dict   = (dict) Option dict from instantiated parser object from validateSS.get_options method.
    - sst_feature   = (str) The SST feature for this object. One of [bf, cp, pp, tf].
    - attr_dict     = (dict) What to populate attribute self.component_dict. Must have at minimum
                        {'mismatch_list': []} so that _compare_fuses method works properly.
        - Keys  | Value
        - {key} | (any) Default value for this key.
    - with_handler  = (bool, optional) If the logger should automatically include the log/file handler. If False, the user
                        should be the one to add the file handler. Default=True.
    - script_path   = (path obj, optional) The path of the script that initializes the instance. This is to help logging keep
                        track of which script is executing when running multiple. Default=None.

Attributes:
    - sockets           = (obj) namednodes component group
    - options           = (dict) Option dict from get_options()
    - feature           = (str) SST feature for this instance
    - _location         = (str) The path for the log
    - logger            = (logger instance) Logger for this class
    - component_dict    = (dict) It's format will be specified by the child. In general:
        - Key           | Value
        - {socket_name} | (dict) All dies of this socket.
            - Key           | Value
            - {die_name}    | (dict) Populated by arg attr_dict.
                - Key           | Value
                - capability    | (dict) Always included. Updated by get_all_capability() and see
                                    function for format.
                - fuse_matches  | (dict) Always included. Updated by check_all_fuses() and see
                                    function for format.
    - boot_level        = (int) The PP level that was booted to

methods.name: get_options
methods.doc:
Creates options parser for validateSS() instantiation.

Static Method.

Current options:
    --testnum, --loglevel, --incname, --dynamicpp, --biospp, --stubmode, --sleeptime, --consolelevel, --overridecap, --cbbonly

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    (obj) Parser object

methods.name: add_additional_handler
methods.doc:
Add addtional handler to the logger of this class.

Args:
    - log_file_path = (str) Name/path of the log file. Must end in .log
    - log_level     = (int or str) The logging level for the log
    - include_name  = (bool, optional) If the log message should include the logger name. Default=False.

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: iterate_over_components
methods.doc:
Will apply the specified function/method across all components.

The function must have the socket and die names as args and return an int. Will skip applying the
function over dies that are not in the filter_list. If you are testing functionality in stub mode,
set run_only_once to True.

Args:
    - rep_function  = (function or method) Function/method that will be executed repeatedly on each component. The
                        function/method must have the format func(socket_name, die_name) or func(socket_name, die_name, rep_arg).
                        The function/method should return an int which is the count of errors that occured on execution.
    - filter_list   = (list of str, optional) Will only apply the function if the die name is in this list.
                        Default=['imh', 'cbb'].
    - run_only_once = (bool, optional) If instead of iterating over all components, apply the specified function only on the very
                        first component. Default=False.
    - rep_args      = (any, optional) Any additional arg for rep_function. Default=None.

Returns:
    (int) The cumulative sum of all int returns from each iteration of rep_function

methods.name: iterate_over_sockets
methods.doc:
Will apply the specified function/method across all sockets.

The function must have the socket name as an arg and return an int.

Args:
    - rep_function  = (function or method) Function/method that will be executed repeatedly on each socket. The function/method
                        must have the format func(socket_name). The function/method should return an int which is the count of
                        errors that occured on execution.
    - rep_args      = (any, optional) Any additional arg for rep_function. Default=None.

Returns:
    (int) The cumulative sum of all int returns from each iteration of rep_function

methods.name: get_all_capability
methods.doc:
Will get the capability of all components and update attribute self.component_dict()

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    No return value

functions.name: parse_die_name
functions.doc:
Parses the die (or socket or core) into the word and number. For example, die0 -> (die, 0)

Args:
    - die_name = (str) Die name to parse

Returns: (tuple)
    - (str) The type of die,
    - (str) The index of the die

functions.name: apply_mask
functions.doc:
Will strip the bits outside the specified mask, then right shift to get value.

Example:
    - upper_bit = 15, lower_bit = 8
    - value  = 0xa623cd
    - mask   = 0x00ff00
    - and    = 0x002300
    - return = 0x000023

Note that this function returns an int. Your usage may need to convert to hex() after call.

Args:
    - value     = (int) Value to apply mask on
    - upper_bit = (int or str) Upper bit of mask. All bits above this limit are set to 0.
    - lower_bit = (int or str) Lower bit of mask. All bits below this limit are set to 0.

Returns:
    (int) The value after masked

functions.name: remove_mask
functions.doc:
Will shift the value to fit in the mask.

Example:
    - upper_bit = 15, lower_bit = 8
    - value  = 0x45ab23
    - mask   = 0x00ff00
    - return = 0x002300

Note that this function returns an int. Your usage may need to convert to hex() after call.

Args:
    - value     = (int) Value to remove mask on
    - upper_bit = (int or str) Upper bit of mask. All bits above this limit are set to 0.
    - lower_bit = (int or str) Lower bit of mask. All bits below this limit are set to 0.

Returns:
    (int) The value after mask removed

functions.name: build_bins
functions.doc:
Will turn a list of bin boundaries into their ranges. For example:
- bound_list = [10, 12, 20, 5]
- returns = {0:(0, 10), 1:(11, 12), 2:(13, 20)}

If the lowest boundary is negative, will return an empty dict {}.

Args:
    - bound_list = (list of int) The highest int of each bin

Returns: (dict) The bins and their ranges. Returns {} if lowest bound is negative.
    - Key           | Value
    - {int of bin}  | (tuple) (int) Lowest value in range, (int) Highest value in range

functions.name: _debug_target_info
functions.doc:
Debug function to compare the target_info on cpu.core#, cbb#.core#, and cbb#.uncore.core_pmsb.core_pmsb#

Args:
    - socket_name = (str) Name of the socket to get info from

Returns:
    No return value

functions.name: time_response
functions.doc:
Measures the time to when the specifiec path changes values.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) Name of the socket that you are getting the value
    - trigger_path  = (str) Namenodes path from the socket of the register or field you wish to change
    - write_val     = (int) Value you wish to write to the trigger path
    - response_path = (str) Namenodes path from the socket of the register or field you wish to track
    - increment     = (float) The number of seconds between each sample
    - max_wait      = (float, optional) The maximum amount of seconds to wait for a response. Default=1.0.

Returns:
    (float) -1.0 if there was no response, otherwise the number of seconds it took to get a response

functions.name: _iterate_on_path
functions.doc:
Prints or writes to all of the values when iterating over a path to help with debug.

Args:
    - skt_num       = (int) The number of the socket to iterate on
    - ini_path      = (str) The start of the path that does not iterate
    - iter_var      = (str) The component/register that is to be iterated over. Should have one of the two following formats:
        - Ends with s, e.g. cores, pmsbs
        - Ends with 0, e.g. io_therm_status_0
    - end_path      = (str) The end of the path that does not iterate. Must start with . e.g. .thread0
    - iter_range    = (range or list) A range() or list that will be iterated
    - action        = (str, optional) One of [print, write]. Either to read the values or attempt to write. Default=print.
    - write_val     = (int, optional) If action=write, what value to write to the path. Default=0.

The final path will look like:
    - If iter_var ends with s:
        namednodes.sv.socket{skt_num}.{ini_path}.{iter_var}{end_path}
    - If iter_var ends with 0:
        namednodes.sv.socket{skt_num}.{ini_path}.{iter_var[:-1]}{i in iter_range}{end_path}

Returns:
    No return value

functions.name: _get_field_bits
functions.doc:
Get field bits from specified register.

Args:
    - feature   = (str) SST feature the register belongs to. One of [header, bf, cp, pp, tf].
    - register  = (str) Register to check field
    - subfield  = (str) Field to get bits from

Returns:
    - None if there is any error, otherwise
    - (tuple of int) Bits of the field (upper bit, lower bit)

functions.name: _get_access_of_reg
functions.doc:
Will get the access of the specified TPMI register

Args:
    - feature   = (str) The feature the register belongs to. One of [header, pp, cp, bf, tf].
    - register  = (str) The name of the register

Returns: (str) '' if there is any error, otherwise
    - 'ro' if the register has read only access
    - 'rw' if the register has read/write access

functions.name: _get_field_from_reg
functions.doc:
Wil apply the field mask on the specified data.

Args:
    - value     = (int) The data from the register to parse.
    - feature   = (str) The feature the register comes from.
    - register  = (str) The name of the register the value comes from.
    - subfield  = (str) The name of the field of the register to parse from the data.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise the value of the specified field.

functions.name: build_dict_from_read
functions.doc:
Will apply _get_field_from_reg() to each field in the specified dict.

Will read the specified register for the subfields in mapping_dict. Will then return the read value
in a dictionary with the same keys as mapping_dict.

Args:
    - feature       = (str) SST feature the register belongs to. One of [header, bf, cp, pp, tf].
    - register      = (str) Register to get subfields to parse data.
    - reg_data      = (int) Data to parse.
    - mapping_dict  = (dict) Mapping dict of key to subfield.
        - Key           | Value
        - {anything}    | (str) Name of the subfield to get from the specified register.

Returns: (dict)
    - Keys                          | Value
    - {same keys as mapping_dict}   | (int) Value of the register of mapping_dict[key]

functions.name: build_default_dict
functions.doc:
Will apply _get_field_from_reg() to each field of the specified register and return the data as a dict.

Args:
    - feature   = (str) SST feature the register belongs to. One of [header, bf, cp, pp, tf].
    - register  = (str) Register to get subfields to parse data.
    - reg_data  = (int) Data to parse.

Returns:
    - None if there is any error, otherwise
    - (dict)
        - Keys          | Value
        - {field name}  | (int) Value of that field

functions.name: build_value_for_write
functions.doc:
Will use a dict of fields and values to build the value for register writing.

If a field does not exist in the specified register, it will not be added to the return value.

Args:
    - feature   = (str) Feature the register belongs to. One of [header, cp, pp, bf, tf].
    - register  = (str) Name of the register that will be written to.
    - data_dict = (dict) Mapping of specific data to its corresponding field.
        - Key           | Value
        - {field_name}  | (int) Data for that field.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise the value for writing.

functions.name: tpmi_read
functions.doc:
Use TPMI to get the value of a register or field.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) Socket name to access
    - die_name      = (str) Die of specified socket to access
    - register      = (str) Name of the register you wish to read from
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) PP-level for info registers. One of [range(5)]. Default=0.
    - subfield      = (str, optional) Name of the field from the register you wish to read from. If Default='', then will return
                        the entire register.

Returns:
    - (int) -1 if there are any errors or if the die does not have a TPMI mailbox, otherwise the value of that register or field.

functions.name: tpmi_write
functions.doc:
Use TPMI to write a value to a register.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) Socket name of access
    - die_name      = (str) Die of specified socket to access
    - register      = (str) Name of the register you wish to write to
    - feature       = (str) SST feature the register belongs to. One of [header, bf, cp, pp, tf].
    - write_value   = (int, required if not supplying write_dict) Value to write to the register or field
    - subfield      = (str, optional) Subfield of the register you wish to write to. If Default='', will write to the whole register.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) If writing to an INFO reg, the pp level of that reg. Default=0.
    - write_dict    = (dict, optional) If specified, will ignore write_value and will instead build the write value from the dict.
                        See diamondrapids.pm.sstUtils.build_value_for_write() function for format.
    - skip_check    = (bool, optional) Will skip the Read-Only check to write. You should only do this in stub mode. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, 0 otherwise.

functions.name: _fill_in_mask
functions.doc:
Will change the bits within the mask to the specified fill value.

Example:
    - upper_bit = 15, lower_bit = 8, fill_value = 1
    - value  = 0xa623cd
    - mask   = 0x00ff00
    - return = 0xa6ffcd

Args:
    - value         = (int) Value to apply mask on
    - upper_bit     = (int or str) Upper bit of mask. All bits above this limit are set to 0.
    - lower_bit     = (int or str) Lower bit of mask. All bits below this limit are set to 0.
    - fill_value    = (int, optional) What to fill the bits with. One of [0, 1]. Default=1.

Returns:
    (int) The value after filling the mask.

functions.name: _fit_field_in_value
functions.doc:
Will get the bits of the specified subfield of the specified register, to then insert the
fit_value into the value.

Example:
    - upper_bit = 15, lower_bit = 8, fill_value = 0x2a
    - value  = 0xa6c9cd
    - mask   = 0x00ff00
    - return = 0xa62acd

Args:
    - value     = (int) The value that is the rest of the register
    - fit_value = (int) The value that you want to put into the subfield
    - register  = (str) The name of the register. Should be a PP register or a CP register since these are the only
                    features with writable registers.
    - subfield  = (str) The name of the field of the specified register

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there is any error, otherwise the field inserted into the value

functions.name: write_to_tpmi_via_oob
functions.doc:
Using OOB, write to the TPMI registers used for SST.

Args:
    - write_val     = (int) The value to write to the register
    - socket_name   = (str) The name of the socket to write to
    - die_name      = (str) The name of the die of the specified socket
    - feature       = (str) The SST feature that the intended register belongs to
    - register      = (str) The name of the register to write to
    - subfield      = (str, optional) The name of the subfield to write to. If Default='', will write to whole register.

Returns:
    (int) 1 if there is any error, otherwise 0

functions.name: get_die_instance
functions.doc:
Will get the instance of the die in the socket.

Args:
    - core      = (RegisterComponent or int, optional) The core to get the instance from. Default=0.
    - die_name  = (str) The name of the die to get the instance from. Default=all

Returns int or dict:
    (int)  The instance of the die
    (dict) All instances of all dies

functions.name: are_fuses_loaded
functions.doc:
Will check if the fuses are loaded on the specified socket+die

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) Name of the socket to check if fuses are loaded
    - die_name      = (str) Name of the die of the specified socket

Returns:
    (bool) Whether the fuses are loaded

functions.name: get_valid_pp_levels
functions.doc:
Will get the valid PP levels for the specified socket+die.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the levels. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (list) - A list of valid PP levels

functions.name: get_lowest_pp_level
functions.doc:
Will look at the fuses to see what is the lowest PP level on the socket.

Args:
    socket_name = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the level. Default=socket0.
    die_name = (str, optional) Name of the die to get the level. Default=imh0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if the fuse is zero, otherwise the lowest level on the socket

functions.name: get_boot_pp_level
functions.doc:
Will read the BIOS knobs and see what the boot PP level is.

Args:
    No args

Returns: (int) -1 if any error, otherwise
    - if the BIOS tried to boot to an invalid PP level: current PP level + 5 (5 = largest level + 1)
    - otherwise the PP level booted to

functions.name: get_capability
functions.doc:
Will get the SST capabilities of the specified socket+die.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get info from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=io0.

Returns: (dict)
    - Key   | Value
    - cp    | (bool) If CP capable
    - pp    | (dict) PP and dynamic capability
        - Key           | Value
        - cap           | (bool) If PP capable
        - dyn           | (bool) If dynamic PP level switching is enabled (sst_pp_level_lock)
        - fdyn          | (bool) If dynamic PP capable from fusing
        - {int 0 to 4}  | (bool) If this PP level is enabled to switch to. All will be 0 if dynamic disabled.
    - bf    | (dict) The BF capability of each PP-level
        - Key           | Value
        - {int 0 to 4}  | (bool) If BF is capable at this level
    - tf    | (dict) The TF capability of each PP-level
        - Key           | Value
        - {int 0 to 4}  | (bool) If TF is capable at this level

functions.name: get_all_sst_regs
functions.doc:
Will use tpmi_read() on all fields/registers.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read regs from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=imh0.
    - tpmi_access   = (str, optional) The type of access to use. One of ['mailbox', 'inband', 'oob']. Default=mailbox.

Returns: (dict) Values of the subfields of all registers for SST. {} if any error.
    - Keys      | Value
    - {feature} | (dict) Registers belonging to this feature.
        - Keys              | Value
        - {register_name}   | (dict) Subfields of this register.
            - Keys          | Value
            - {field_name}  | (int or str) Current value of that field or 'missing' if unable to be read.
            - {level_name}  | (dict) Field values of the register by PP-level (for info regs).
                - Keys          | Value
                - {field_name}  | (int or str) Same as above.

functions.name: get_reg_from_all_dies
functions.doc:
Will use tpmi_read() on all sockets+dies for a specific register.

Args:
    - feature   = (str) Name of the feature the register belongs to. One of [header, pp, cp, bf, tf].
    - reg_name  = (str) Name of the register to get from all dies.
    - pp_level  = (int, optional) PP-level if accessing an info reg. One of [range(5)]. Default=0.

Returns: (dict) Values of registers. {} if there are any errors
    - Key           | Value
    - {socket_name} | (dict) Dict of all dies.
        - Key           | Value
        - {die_name}    | (dict) Dict of subfields.
            - Key               | Value
            - {subfield_name}   | (int) Value of that subfield of the socket+die. -1 if could not get.

functions.name: get_module_list
functions.doc:
Will get the names of all cores on a cbb die. Return value is different depending on if bigcore or atom.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the cores from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    - None if there are any errors,
    - otherwise if bigcore: (list of instances) the modules of the cbb die
    - otherwise if atom: (dict)
        - Key              | Value
        - module instance  | (list of str) Cores of the module

functions.name: get_core_list
functions.doc:
Will get the names of all cores on a cbb die. Return value is different depending on if bigcore or atom.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the cores from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    - None if there are any errors,
    - otherwise if bigcore: (list of instances) the cores of the cbb die
    - otherwise if atom: (dict)
        - Key            | Value
        - core instance  | (list of str) Cores of the module

functions.name: get_num_cores
functions.doc:
Will get the number of all available cores of a cbb die.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the count from. Default='socket0'
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. If Default=all, will get the total cores on the socket.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise the number of cores on the specified die.

functions.name: check_hwp_interrupt
functions.doc:
Will check the reg uncore.punit.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.hwp_status if an interrupt occured.

During SST-PP and SST-BF operations, Pcode should raise the interrupt hwp.change_to_guaranteed. Note that SW may clear this reg after reading it,
Pcode does not clear the reg itself. This function will clear the reg to match this behavior.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to check the interrupt. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - clear_reg     = (bool, optional) If the function should clear the reg after reading. Default=True.

Returns: (dict) The interrupts from all cores
    - Key               | Value
    - {int of core PID} | (bool) True if there was an interrupt, false otherwise

functions.name: force_turbo_enabled
functions.doc:
Enable turbo on specified socket+die. This is done by writing to CR ucode_cr_perf_ctl.turbo_dis on core0.thread0 on the
die. A change to a single CR on any core/thread should control turbo on all cores.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str) Name of the socket to change enable turbo on
    - die_name          = (str) Name of the die of the specified socket
    - disable_instead   = (bool, optional) If turbo should be disabled by this method instead of enabled. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) 1 if turbo state was changed, otherwise 0

functions.name: _get_turbo_status
functions.doc:
Will get the turbo status of the specified socket.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get turbo status. Default=socket0.
    - info_method   = (str, optional) The method to get turbo status. One of [cr, bios]. Default cr.

Returns:
    (bool) True if turbo is enabled, False otherwise.

functions.name: print_info_table
functions.doc:
Will get all the TPMI info registers for SST from the specified socket+die, and will print an organized table to help
with debugging.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to read info registers from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die of the specified socket. Default=io0.
    - feature       = (str, optional) Which SST feature to get info from. One of [pp, bf, tf]. If Default='', will print for all.
    - int_spacing   = (int, optional) How much space to give every int. Minimium is 5. Default=12.
    - print_zeros   = (bool, optional) If zero values should be printed. Default=True.

Returns:
    1 if any error, otherwise 0

functions.name: _set_pp_ctl_on_die
functions.doc:
Will use TPMI to write to register sst_pp_control of the specified socket+die. Note that expectation is that all dies get
the same PP level (not enforced).

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str) Name of the socket to assign data to.
    - die_name      = (str) Name of the die of the specified socket.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) PP-level you want this component to go to. Must be in _VALID_PP_LEVEL. Default=0.
    - bf_en         = (bool, optional) If BF will be enabled for the PP-level to go to. Default=False.
    - tf_en         = (bool, optional) If TF will be enabled for the PP-level to go to. Default=False.
    - pp_lock       = (bool, optional) If the level assignment will be locked until next reset. Default=False.
    - oob           = (bool, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use OOB to write to the registers. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if there are any errors, otherwise 0.

functions.name: _get_assignment_list
functions.doc:
Assignment order for SST control. Must go as follows (read more TPMI LUT): imh0 -> cbb0 [-> cbb1 -> cbb2] -> imh1

Args:
    - socket_name = (str) Name of the socket to read subcomponents

Returns:
    (list of str) The die order for assignment

functions.name: set_pp_ctl
functions.doc:
Sets the SST state on TPMI sst_pp_control on all dies.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to set feature state. Default=socket0.
    - pp_level      = (int, optional) PP-level you want this component to go to. Must be in _VALID_PP_LEVEL. If Default=-1, will use current PP level.
    - bf_en         = (int, optional) 1 if BF will be enabled for the PP-level to go to. If Default=-1, will keep current BF setting.
    - tf_en         = (int, optional) 1 if TF will be enabled for the PP-level to go to. If Default=-1, will keep current TF setting.
    - pp_lock       = (bool, optional) If the level assignment will be locked until next reset. Default=False.
    - oob           = (bool, optional) Instead of writing directly to registers, use OOB to write to the registers. Default=False.

Returns:
    (int) The number of failed assignments, otherwise 0.

functions.name: is_turbo_enabled
functions.doc:
Will read from pcudata on all cbbs of the specified socket to get resolved turbo disable and disable reasons. If any
of the cbbs say they are disabled, will report that the whole socket is turbo disabled.

Args:
    - socket_name = (str) Name of the socket to check if turbo is enabled

Returns:
    (int) 1 if turbo is enabled, 0 otherwise

functions.name: _create_sst_logger
functions.doc:
Creates a unique logger for all SST scripts.

Args:
    - feature       = (str, optional) The SST feature that will be in the logger name. One of [pp, cp, bf, tf]. Default='pp'.
                        If input is not in the valid set, will use 'pp'.
    - with_handler  = (bool, optional) Make the logger with handlers. Default=True.
    - log_level     = (str, optional) The level for the log file. One of [debug, sample, info, warning, error, result, critical,
                        header]. Default='info'.
    - console_level = (str, optional) The level of console prints. One of the same list for arg log_level. Default='result'.
    - inc_name      = (bool, optional) If you want log file to also print the logger name. Default=False.
    - new_path      = (str, optional) The specific path for the log file. If Default='', will create log at path
                        _LOG_PATH.sst_test_###.log

Returns:
    (logger instance) Instance that you could get from logging.getLogger()

functions.name: compare_info_dict
functions.doc:
Will compare info dicts of a feature.

The keys of all dicts must be the same. Will go through by key and do the following comparison:
    current_value {comparison_value} target_value
If the comparison is False, will append that information to the return list.

Usage:
    - current={'tpd':8}
    - target={'tdp':6}
    - comparison={'tdp':'<='}
    - compare_info_dict(current, target, comparison)
    - returns: ['tdp is greater than target']

Args:
    - current_dict      = (dict) Current info of the device.
    - target_dict       = (dict) The target values for all specified info.
    - comparison_dict   = (dict) The necessary comparisons for all values.

Returns:
    (list of str) Strings are descriptions of all mismatches.

module_doc:
Module to validate SST parameters that impact multiple features for DMR

Functions:

Classes:

Main in the file

classes.name: coreHistory
classes.doc:
Class which holds the history of the core frequencies on all sockets and all dies.

Args:
    - map_obj       = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.
    - max_sample    = (int, optional) The maximum number of samples to store. Default=10.
    - sample_method = (str, optional) The method to get the core frequencies. One of [sb, iowp]. Default=sb.

Methods:
    - sample_freq   :sample the freq of all cores, and update the state attribute
    - get_latest    :get the core freq from the latest sample
    - get_average   :get the average core freq from all samples in the state attribute

methods.name: __init__
methods.doc:
Attributes:
- map_obj           = (coreMap object) Core mapping
- max_sample        = (int) The maximum number of values to store
- _sample_method    = (str) The method to get core frequency. One of [ucode_cr, msr]
- state             = (dict) All information that is stored in this object
    - Key                   | Value
    - {str of socket name}  | (dict) All cbbs on this socket
        - Key                   | Value
        - {str of cbb name} | (dict) All cores on this cbb
            - Key                   | Value
            - {str of core name}    | (dict) Info on this core
                - Key   | Value
                - id    | (tuple of ints) The cores PID, LID
                - hist  | (list of int) The past 10 samples of the core's frequency
- samples           = (int) The current number of samples that have been taken. Will be in range(0,10).

methods.name: sample_freq
methods.doc:
Reads the current frequency of the cores and updates attribute state

Args:
    No args

Returns:
    No return value

methods.name: get_latest
methods.doc:
From attribute state, gets the latest sample. If you wish to see all samples taken, read the state attribute directly.

Args:
    No args

Returns: (dict) The latest frequencies
    - Key                   | Value
    - {str of socket name}  | (dict) All cbbs on this socket
        - Key                   | Value
        - {str of cbb name} | (dict) All cores on this cbb
            - Key                   | Value
            - {str of core name}    | (int) The frequency of this core from the latest sample

methods.name: get_average
methods.doc:
From attribute state, gets the average frequency amongst all samples.

Args:
    No args

Returns: (dict) The average frequencies
    - Key                   | Value
    - {str of socket name}  | (dict) All cbbs on this socket
        - Key                   | Value
        - {str of cbb name} | (dict) All cores on this cbb
            - Key                   | Value
            - {str of core name}    | (float) The average frequency of this core

functions.name: _get_guaranteed_ratio
functions.doc:
Will read the pcudata of the specified socket+die+core to get its assigned guaranteed ratio. If atom core, will get
guaranteed ratio of the atom module.

Args:
    - core_num      = (int) Number of the core of the specified cbb die if bigcore. Number of the atom module if atom cores.
                        Pcudata enumerates this variable by physical ID.
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the ratio from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if the core num does not exist, otherwise the guaranteed ratio

functions.name: _get_max_ratio
functions.doc:
Will read the pcudata of the specified socket+die+core to get its assigned maximum ratio. If atom core, will get max ratio of the atom module.

Args:
    - core_num      = (int) Max ratio of the core of the specified cbb die if bigcore. Max of the atom module if atom core.
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get the ratio from. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.

Returns:
    (int) -1 if the core num does not have a PID, otherwise the maximum ratio

functions.name: check_bf_p1
functions.doc:
Will see if the P1 frequencies for SST-BF are set correctly on the pcudata variables. Will see which cores are online at
current PP level (sst_pp_info_1). Will see which cores are high priority at current PP level (sst_bf_info_1).
- Offline: p1 == sst_pp_info_11.pm_core_ratio
- Online + hi: p1 == sst_bf_info_0.p1_hi
- Online + lo: p1 == sst_bf_info_0.p1_lo

Note that SST-CP supersedes P1 set by BF; the core will always obey the minimum frequency of the core's CLOS group in TPMI
sst_clos_conig_#. Thus if SST-CP is enabled, a warning will be issued since the target values for each core will be wrong.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level          = (int, optional) If Default=-1, will automatically get PP level. Otherwise the level to get TPMI info.
    - return_messages   = (str, optional) What messages to return. One of the following. Default=none.
        - none  = have no messages returned
        - error = have only errors returned
        - all   = have both successes and errors returned
    - map_obj           = (coreMap object, optional) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.
    - log                = (Logger object, optional) The logger to use. If Default=_log, will use the logger set up in this module.

Returns: (int)
    - (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, otherwise the number of cores that were incorrectly set

functions.name: check_pp_p1
functions.doc:
Will see if the P1 frequencies for SST-PP are set correctly on the pcudata variables. Will see which cores are online at
current PP level (sst_pp_info_1). Online cores must have frequency == sst_pp_info_11.p1_core_ratio. Offline cores must have
frequency == sst_pp_info_11.pm_core_ratio. The pcudata variables on boot seem to be populated with flex ratio, but will change
to the right values on dynamic SST states.

If SST-BF is enabled, a different check will be called (see check_bf_p1). If atom core, will always do PP check since SRF
does not have SST-BF.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level          = (int, optional) If Default=-1, will automatically get PP level. Otherwise the level to get TPMI info.
    - return_messages   = (str, optional) What messages to return. One of the following. Default=none.
        - none  = have no messages returned
        - error = have only errors returned
        - all   = have both successes and errors returned
    - map_obj           = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns: (tuple)
    - (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, otherwise the number of cores that were incorrectly set
    - (list of str) The return messages, depending on arg return_messages
        - none  = no messages, will be an empty list []
        - error = only error messages, will be an empty list if there are no errors
        - all   = all messages, both passes and failures

functions.name: check_core_max
functions.doc:
Will see if the maximum frequencies for SST-PP are set correctly on the pcudata variables. Enablment/disablment
or turbo, SST-TF, and SST-BF will impact the target values.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level          = (int, optional) If Default=-1, will automatically get PP level. Otherwise the level to get TPMI info.
    - return_messages   = (str, optional) What messages to return. One of the following. Default=none.
        - none  = have no messages returned
        - error = have only errors returned
        - all   = have both successes and errors returned
    - map_obj           = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns: (tuple)
    - (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, otherwise the number of cores that were incorrectly set
    - (list of str) The return messages, depending on arg return_messages
        - none  = no messages, will be an empty list []
        - error = only error messages, will be an empty list if there are no errors
        - all   = all messages, both passes and failures

functions.name: _get_current_freq
functions.doc:
Will get the current frequency of the specified core.

Args:
    - module_num    = (RegisterComponent | int) Module of the specified die if big core. Module number of the specified die if atom core. When using iowp, expects
                        LID. When using sb, expects PID.
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the specified socket to get core frequency. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - source_method = (str, optional) The source of core frequencies. One of [msr, ucode_cr]. Default=msr.

Returns: -1 if there is any error, otherwise the frequency of the core

functions.name: average_freq
functions.doc:
Will get the average core frequency for each core on the specified socket+die

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the specified socket to get core frequencies. Default=socket0.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - samples       = (int, optional) The number of samples to take, that are 0.25 sec apart. Default=40.
    - map_obj       = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.

Returns: (dict) The average frequencies of each core
    - Key               | Value
    - {int PID of core} | (float) Average frequency of the core

functions.name: check_freq_in_range
functions.doc:
Will see if the current frequencies from MSR 0x198 are within min/max for SST-PP. Enablment/disablment
or turbo, SST-TF, and SST-BF will impact the target values.

Args:
    - socket_name       = (str, optional) Name of the socket to do this check on. Default=socket0.
    - die_name          = (str, optional) Name of the cbb die of the specified socket. Default=cbb0.
    - pp_level          = (int, optional) If Default=-1, will automatically get PP level. Otherwise the level to get TPMI info.
    - return_method     = (str, optional) One of the following. Default=none.
        - none  = no message should be returned
        - error = only errors should be returned
        - all   = the frequency of all cores should be returned
    - map_obj           = (coreMap object) The core mapping to use. If Default=None, will automatically get a mapping.
    - hist_obj          = (coreHistory object) The history of all core frequencies if you want to check by averages. If
                            Default=None, will do normal one time sample. Otherwise will get frequency from this object.

Returns:
    - If return_method == none:
        (int) -1 if TPMI reg cannot be accessed, otherwise the number of cores that were incorrectly set
    - If return_method == error:
        (tuple) (int) Above, (list of str) The list of all mismatched cores
    - If return_method == all:
        (tuple) (int) Above, (list of str) The frequencies of all cores

functions.name: sst_debug
functions.doc:
Debug script to run when triaging some bug with SST.

Args:
    - socket_name   = (str, optional) Name of the socket to get relevant information from. If Default='', will iterate over all
                        sockets.
    - die_name      = (str, optional) Name of the die on the specified socket. If Default='', will iterate over all dies on the
                        specified socket.
    - return_table  = (bool, optional) If the table should be returned. Default=False.
    - sample_method = (str, optional) What is the sample method for getting current frequency. One of [msr, ucode_cr]. Default=ucode_cr.

Returns:
    - If return_table == False, no return value.
    - Else, (list of str) All debug info

module_doc:
Owner - amit6.kumar@intel.com

Socket rapl debug tool script for sweeping RAPL limits and analyzing power response in the graphical representation.

Features
--------
* Accepts a run duration (seconds) and sweeps fast RAPL limits for that window.
* Launches the PTAT/PTU workload (`./ptat -ct 5 -id`) from `/usr/local/ptat`.
* Samples package power and core ratio at mode-specific cadences (fast=500 microseconds, PL1=1 second, PL2=10 milliseconds).
* Highlights any limit window where actual power never reaches 90% of the target.
* Emits a Matplotlib plot summarizing power, limits, and highlighted violations.

methods.name: __settime__
methods.doc:
Encode a desired time (ms) into the (x,y) RAPL time-window fields and program it.

The RAPL time-window is encoded as two fields: x in [0..3] and y in [0..31]
where the actual window is (1 + x/4) * 2**y. This function finds the nearest
representable (x,y) pair for the requested `timev` and writes the encoded
value via CSR or TPMI depending on `method`.

Args:
    pl: power limit index (1 or 2)
    timev: requested time window in milliseconds (float)
    sktNum: socket index
    method: access method string ('csr'|'tpmi'|'all')

methods.name: _verify_pl_convergence
methods.doc:
Generic helper to verify convergence to a programmed platform power limit.

Args:
    sock: namednodes socket object
    limit: target power limit (watts) that was programmed
    pl_tau_ms: programmed tau (ms) for the limit
    pl_index: 1 for PPL1, 2 for PPL2 (used for logging)
    method: access method string (kept for future use)

Returns True on convergence within 5*Tau and the configured tolerance.
In verify mode failures will call self.print_and_exit.

methods.name: verify_ppl1_convergence
methods.doc: Wrapper for verifying PPL1 convergence.

methods.name: verify_ppl2_convergence
methods.doc: Wrapper for verifying PPL2 convergence.

methods.name: request_pstate
methods.doc: Requesting P state through Pega Commands

methods.name: pem_decode
methods.doc:
Decode a PEM status bitmask (or a node-like object) into reason names.

Accepts:
  - an int bitmask
  - a namednode-like object with a .get_value() method
Returns a list of reason strings (may be empty).

module_doc:
Call the provided functions with appropriate socket and IMH indices to perform the desired verification or retrieve configuration information.
Team: PM 
Sub-Team: PM Active 
Owner(s): Patricia Canete Leyva
email: patricia.canete.leyva@intel.com 

Intent of the script: 
This script provides a suite of verification and telemetry functions for SVID (Serial Voltage Identification) power management on Intel Diamond Rapids platforms. 
It is intended for use by the Power Management (PM) Active sub-team to validate and retrieve configuration and runtime telemetry data for voltage regulators (VRs) and power domains.
Key functionalities include:
- Verifying the presence and configuration of MBVRs (Multi-Bus Voltage Regulators) via fuse and PCU data.
- Checking poll priorities and address mappings for VRs.
- Calculating VR power based on telemetry and configuration data.
- Reading and verifying PMON (power monitor) and IMON (current monitor) masks and accumulators.
- Sampling and comparing telemetry data from multiple sources (SVID, Punit, PcodeIO, Pcudata).
- Generating tabulated reports for PMON and IMON telemetry.
- Calculating and comparing socket-level power using RAPL (Running Average Power Limit) energy status and other telemetry sources.

      written around the SVID telemetry APIs (diamondrapids/pm/svid.py).
      This code handles all the exceptions and return value compatible with NGA.p

functions.name: verify_DDR_VR_PMON_address
functions.doc:
Verify DDR VR PMON address is valid or not

Returns : SUCCESS or FAILURE depending on b2p data received validation on
          DDR_MISC_CONTROL register.

functions.doc:
Verify DDR VR IMON address is valid or not
(Not supported on GNR)
(Will get return error as illegal subcommand)

Returns : SUCCESS or FAILURE depending on b2p data received validation on
          DDR_MISC_CONTROL register.

functions.name: verify_pmon_telemetry
functions.doc:
PMON telemetry verification

Returns : SUCCESS or FAILURE depending on verification average imon value
          returned.

functions.name: poll_rapl_svid_telemetry
functions.doc:
poll_rapl_svid_telemetry(sktNum=0, die='imh0', delay=1)

svid_i_out_accumulator - Vccin MBVR output current telemetry data accumulated over time
svid_p_in_accumulator - Vccin MBVR input power telemetry data accumulated over time
svid_i_out_num_samples_raw - Running counter of the number of times that output current telemetry has been sampled/accumulated from an MBVR
svid_p_in_num_samples_raw - Running counter of the number of times that input power telemetry has been sampled/accumulated from an MBVR
